name: SIGMA - Mimikatz Use
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), David ANDRE (additional keywords)
description: This method detects mimikatz keywords in different Eventlogs (some of them only appear in older Mimikatz version that are however still used by different threat groups)
false_positives: Files with Mimikatz in their filename
query: any where ("dpapi::masterkey" or "eo.oe.kiwi" or "event::clear" or "event::drop" or "gentilkiwi.com" or "kerberos::golden" or "kerberos::ptc" or "kerberos::ptt" or "kerberos::tgt" or "Kiwi Legit Printer" or "lsadump::" or "mimidrv.sys" or "\\mimilib.dll" or "misc::printnightmare" or "misc::shadowcopies" or "misc::skeleton" or "privilege::backup" or "privilege::debug" or "privilege::driver" or "sekurlsa::") and (not EventID:15)

name: SIGMA - File Was Not Allowed To Run
author: Pushkarev Dmitry
description: Detect run not allowed files. Applocker is a very useful tool, especially on servers where unprivileged users have access. For example terminal servers. You need configure applocker and log collect to receive these events.
false_positives: Need tuning applocker or add exceptions in SIEM
query: any where EventID like~ (8004, 8007, 8022, 8025)

name: SIGMA - Sysinternals Tools AppX Versions Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of Sysinternals tools via an AppX package. Attackers could install the Sysinternals Suite to get access to tools such as psexec and procdump to avoid detection based on System paths
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the applications from the Windows Store
query: any where EventID:201 and (ImageName like~ ("procdump.exe", "psloglist.exe", "psexec.exe", "livekd.exe", "ADExplorer.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Deployment AppX Package Was Blocked By AppLocker
author: frack113
description: Detects an appx package deployment that was blocked by AppLocker policy
query: any where EventID:412

name: SIGMA - Potential Malicious AppX Package Installation Attempts
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential installation or installation attempts of known malicious appx packages
false_positives: Rare occasions where a malicious package uses the exact same name and version as a legtimate application
query: any where (EventID like~ (400, 401)) and PackageFullName:"*3669e262-ec02-4e9d-bcb4-3d008b4afac9*"

name: SIGMA - Deployment Of The AppX Package Was Blocked By The Policy
author: frack113
description: Detects an appx package deployment that was blocked by the local computer policy
query: any where EventID like~ (441, 442, 453, 454)

name: SIGMA - Suspicious AppX Package Installation Attempt
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an appx package installation with the error code "0x80073cff" which indicates that the package didn't meet the signing requirements and could be suspicious
false_positives: Legitimate AppX packages not signed by MS used part of an enterprise
query: any where EventID:401 and ErrorCode:"0x80073cff"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Remote AppX Package Locations
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an appx package added to the pipeline of the "to be processed" packages which was downloaded from a suspicious domain.
query: any where EventID:854 and (Path like~ ("*.githubusercontent.com*", "*anonfiles.com*", "*cdn.discordapp.com*", "*ddns.net*", "*dl.dropboxusercontent.com*", "*ghostbin.co*", "*glitch.me*", "*gofile.io*", "*hastebin.com*", "*mediafire.com*", "*mega.nz*", "*onrender.com*", "*pages.dev*", "*paste.ee*", "*pastebin.com*", "*pastebin.pl*", "*pastetext.net*", "*privatlab.com*", "*privatlab.net*", "*send.exploit.in*", "*sendspace.com*", "*storage.googleapis.com*", "*storjshare.io*", "*supabase.co*", "*temp.sh*", "*transfer.sh*", "*trycloudflare.com*", "*ufile.io*", "*w3spaces.com*", "*workers.dev*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious AppX Package Locations
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an appx package added the pipeline of the "to be processed" packages which is located in suspicious locations
query: any where EventID:854 and (Path like~ ("*C:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*/users/public/*", "*C:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*C:/perflogs/*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*/desktop/*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*/Downloads/*", "*C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*C:/Windows/Temp/*", "*\\AppdData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*/AppdData/Local/Temp/*"))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon AppX Package Locations
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an appx package added the pipeline of the "to be processed" packages which is located in uncommon locations
query: any where EventID:854 and (not ((Path like~ ("*C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\*", "*C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\SystemApps\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\PrintDialog\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\ImmersiveControlPanel\\*", "*x-windowsupdate://*", "*file:///C:/Program%20Files*", "*AppData/Local/Temp/WinGet/Microsoft.Winget.Source*")) or (Path like~ ("*https://statics.teams.cdn.live.net/*", "*https://statics.teams.cdn.office.net/*", "*microsoft.com*", "*https://installer.teams.static.microsoft/*", "*https://res.cdn.office.net*")))) and (not Path:"*AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\OneDrive\\*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Digital Signature Of AppX Package
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of AppX packages with known suspicious or malicious signature
query: any where EventID:157 and subjectName:"CN=Foresee Consulting Inc., O=Foresee Consulting Inc., L=North York, S=Ontario, C=CA, SERIALNUMBER=1004913-1, OID.1.3.6.1.4.1.311.60.2.1.3=CA, OID.2.5.4.15=Private Organization"

name: SIGMA - New BITS Job Created Via Bitsadmin
author: frack113
description: Detects the creation of a new bits job by Bitsadmin
false_positives: Many legitimate applications or scripts could leverage "bitsadmin". This event is best correlated with EID 16403 via the JobID field
query: any where EventID:3 and processPath:"*\\bitsadmin.exe"

name: SIGMA - New BITS Job Created Via PowerShell
author: frack113
description: Detects the creation of a new bits job by PowerShell
query: any where EventID:3 and (processPath like~ ("*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe"))

name: SIGMA - BITS Transfer Job Downloading File Potential Suspicious Extension
author: frack113
description: Detects new BITS transfer job saving local files with potential suspicious extensions
false_positives: While the file extensions in question can be suspicious at times. It's best to add filters according to your environment to avoid large amount false positives
query: any where (EventID:16403 and (LocalName like~ ("*.bat", "*.dll", "*.exe", "*.hta", "*.ps1", "*.psd1", "*.sh", "*.vbe", "*.vbs"))) and (not (LocalName:"*\\AppData\\*" and RemoteName:"*.com*"))

name: SIGMA - BITS Transfer Job Download From File Sharing Domains
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects BITS transfer job downloading files from a file sharing domain.
query: any where EventID:16403 and (RemoteName like~ ("*.githubusercontent.com*", "*anonfiles.com*", "*cdn.discordapp.com*", "*ddns.net*", "*dl.dropboxusercontent.com*", "*ghostbin.co*", "*glitch.me*", "*gofile.io*", "*hastebin.com*", "*mediafire.com*", "*mega.nz*", "*onrender.com*", "*pages.dev*", "*paste.ee*", "*pastebin.com*", "*pastebin.pl*", "*pastetext.net*", "*pixeldrain.com*", "*privatlab.com*", "*privatlab.net*", "*send.exploit.in*", "*sendspace.com*", "*storage.googleapis.com*", "*storjshare.io*", "*supabase.co*", "*temp.sh*", "*transfer.sh*", "*trycloudflare.com*", "*ufile.io*", "*w3spaces.com*", "*workers.dev*"))

name: SIGMA - BITS Transfer Job Download From Direct IP
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a BITS transfer job downloading file(s) from a direct IP address.

name: SIGMA - BITS Transfer Job With Uncommon Or Suspicious Remote TLD
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious download using the BITS client from a FQDN that is unusual. Adversaries may abuse BITS jobs to persistently execute or clean up after malicious payloads.
false_positives: This rule doesn't exclude other known TLDs such as ".org" or ".net". It's recommended to apply additional filters for software and scripts that leverage the BITS service
query: any where EventID:16403 and (not (RemoteName like~ ("*.azureedge.net/*", "*.com/*", "*.sfx.ms/*", "*download.mozilla.org/*", "*cdn.onenote.net/*", "*cdn.office.net/*")))

name: SIGMA - BITS Transfer Job Download To Potential Suspicious Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects new BITS transfer job where the LocalName/Saved file is stored in a potentially suspicious location
query: any where EventID:16403 and (LocalName like~ ("*\\Desktop\\*", "*C:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*C:\\PerfLogs\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Certificate Private Key Acquired
author: Zach Mathis
description: Detects when an application acquires a certificate private key
false_positives: Legitimate application requesting certificate exports will trigger this. Apply additional filters as needed
query: any where EventID:70

name: SIGMA - Certificate Exported From Local Certificate Store
author: Zach Mathis
description: Detects when an application exports a certificate (and potentially the private key as well) from the local Windows certificate store.
false_positives: Legitimate application requesting certificate exports will trigger this. Apply additional filters as needed
query: any where EventID:1007

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Unmet Signing Level Requirements By File Under Validation
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects attempted file load events that did not meet the signing level requirements. It often means the file's signature is revoked or a signature with the Lifetime Signing EKU has expired.
This event is best correlated with EID 3089 to determine the error of the validation.
false_positives: Antivirus and other third party products are known to trigger this rule quite a lot. Initial filters and tuning is required before using this rule.
query: any where (EventID like~ (3033, 3034)) and (not (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Windows\\assembly\\GAC\\*" and ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\mscorsvw.exe" and ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\Windows\\Microsoft.NET\\*" and RequestedPolicy:8)) and (not ((FileNameBuffer:"*\\Program Files\\DTrace\\dtrace.dll" and ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe" and RequestedPolicy:12) or (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Windows\\System32\\DriverStore\\FileRepository\\*" and FileNameBuffer:"*\\igd10iumd64.dll" and RequestedPolicy:7) or (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Windows\\System32\\nvspcap64.dll" and (ProcessNameBuffer like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Keybase\\Gui\\Keybase.exe", "*\\Microsoft\\Teams\\stage\\Teams.exe")) and RequestedPolicy:8) or (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Program Files\\Bonjour\\mdnsNSP.dll" and (ProcessNameBuffer like~ ("*\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe", "*\\Windows\\System32\\SIHClient.exe")) and (RequestedPolicy like~ (8, 12))) or (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Microsoft Office\\root\\vfs\\ProgramFilesCommonX64\\Microsoft Shared\\OFFICE*" and FileNameBuffer:"*\\MSOXMLMF.DLL" and RequestedPolicy:7) or (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Windows\\System32\\nvspcap64.dll" and ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\AppData\\Local\\slack\\app-*" and ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\slack.exe" and RequestedPolicy:8) or ((FileNameBuffer like~ ("*\\Mozilla Firefox\\mozavcodec.dll", "*\\Mozilla Firefox\\mozavutil.dll")) and ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe" and RequestedPolicy:8) or ((FileNameBuffer like~ ("*\\Program Files\\Avast Software\\Avast\\aswAMSI.dll", "*\\Program Files (x86)\\Avast Software\\Avast\\aswAMSI.dll")) and (RequestedPolicy like~ (8, 12))) or (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Program Files\\Google\\Drive File Stream\\*" and FileNameBuffer:"*\\crashpad_handler.exe" and ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\Windows\\ImmersiveControlPanel\\SystemSettings.exe" and RequestedPolicy:8) or (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Trend Micro\\Client Server Security Agent\\perficrcperfmonmgr.dll" and RequestedPolicy:8) or FileNameBuffer:"*\\Program Files\\National Instruments\\Shared\\mDNS Responder\\nimdnsNSP.dll " or (FileNameBuffer like~ ("*\\Program Files\\McAfee\\Endpoint Security\\Threat Prevention\\MfeAmsiProvider.dll", "*\\Program Files\\McAfee\\MfeAV\\AMSIExt.dll")) or FileNameBuffer:"*\\Program Files\\ESET\\ESET Security\\eamsi.dll" or FileNameBuffer:"*\\Program Files\\comodo\\comodo internet security\\amsiprovider_x64.dll" or (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Program Files\\SentinelOne\\Sentinel Agent*" or ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\Program Files\\SentinelOne\\Sentinel Agent*") or FileNameBuffer:"*\\National Instruments\\Shared\\mDNS Responder\\*" or ((ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\Kaspersky Lab\\*" and ProcessNameBuffer:"*\\avp.exe*") or (FileNameBuffer:"*\\Kaspersky Lab\\*" and FileNameBuffer:"*\\antimalware_provider.dll*"))))

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Disallowed File For Protected Processes Has Been Blocked
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects block events for files that are disallowed by code integrity for protected processes
query: any where EventID:3104

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Blocked Image/Driver Load For Policy Violation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects blocked load events that did not meet the authenticode signing level requirements or violated the code integrity policy.
query: any where EventID:3077

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Blocked Driver Load With Revoked Certificate
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects blocked load attempts of revoked drivers
query: any where EventID:3023

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Revoked Kernel Driver Loaded
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the load of a revoked kernel driver
query: any where EventID like~ (3021, 3022)

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Blocked Image Load With Revoked Certificate
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects blocked image load events with revoked certificates by code integrity.
query: any where EventID:3036

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Revoked Image Loaded
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects image load events with revoked certificates by code integrity.
query: any where EventID like~ (3032, 3035)

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Unsigned Kernel Module Loaded
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the presence of a loaded unsigned kernel module on the system.
query: any where EventID:3001

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Unsigned Image Loaded
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects loaded unsigned image on the system
query: any where EventID:3037

name: SIGMA - CodeIntegrity - Unmet WHQL Requirements For Loaded Kernel Module
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects loaded kernel modules that did not meet the WHQL signing requirements.
query: any where (EventID like~ (3082, 3083)) and (not (FileNameBuffer like~ ("system32\\drivers\\vsock.sys", "System32\\drivers\\vmci.sys")))

name: SIGMA - DNS Query for Anonfiles.com Domain - DNS Client
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects DNS queries for anonfiles.com, which is an anonymous file upload platform often used for malicious purposes
query: any where EventID:3008 and QueryName:"*.anonfiles.com*"

name: SIGMA - DNS Query To MEGA Hosting Website - DNS Client
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects DNS queries for subdomains related to MEGA sharing website
false_positives: Legitimate DNS queries and usage of Mega
query: any where EventID:3008 and QueryName:"*userstorage.mega.co.nz*"

name: SIGMA - DNS Query To Put.io - DNS Client
author: Omar Khaled (@beacon_exe)
description: Detects DNS queries for subdomains related to "Put.io" sharing website.
false_positives: Legitimate DNS queries and usage of Put.io
query: any where EventID:3008 and (QueryName like~ ("*api.put.io*", "*upload.put.io*"))

name: SIGMA - Query Tor Onion Address - DNS Client
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects DNS resolution of an .onion address related to Tor routing networks
query: any where EventID:3008 and (QueryName like~ ("*.hiddenservice.net", "*.onion.ca", "*.onion.cab", "*.onion.casa", "*.onion.city", "*.onion.direct", "*.onion.dog", "*.onion.glass", "*.onion.gq", "*.onion.guide", "*.onion.in.net", "*.onion.ink", "*.onion.it", "*.onion.link", "*.onion.lt", "*.onion.lu", "*.onion.ly", "*.onion.mn", "*.onion.network", "*.onion.nu", "*.onion.pet", "*.onion.plus", "*.onion.pt", "*.onion.pw", "*.onion.rip", "*.onion.sh", "*.onion.si", "*.onion.to", "*.onion.top", "*.onion.ws", "*.onion", "*.s1.tor-gateways.de", "*.s2.tor-gateways.de", "*.s3.tor-gateways.de", "*.s4.tor-gateways.de", "*.s5.tor-gateways.de", "*.t2w.pw", "*.tor2web.ae.org", "*.tor2web.blutmagie.de", "*.tor2web.com", "*.tor2web.fi", "*.tor2web.io", "*.tor2web.org", "*.tor2web.xyz", "*.torlink.co"))

name: SIGMA - DNS Query To Ufile.io - DNS Client
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects DNS queries to "ufile.io", which was seen abused by malware and threat actors as a method for data exfiltration
false_positives: DNS queries for "ufile" are not malicious by nature necessarily. Investigate the source to determine the necessary actions to take
query: any where EventID:3008 and QueryName:"*ufile.io*"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Cobalt Strike DNS Beaconing - DNS Client
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a program that invoked suspicious DNS queries known from Cobalt Strike beacons
query: any where EventID:3008 and ((QueryName like~ ("aaa.stage.*", "post.1*")) or QueryName:"*.stage.123456.*")

name: SIGMA - Failed DNS Zone Transfer
author: Zach Mathis
description: Detects when a DNS zone transfer failed.
query: any where EventID:6004

name: SIGMA - DNS Server Error Failed Loading the ServerLevelPluginDLL
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a DNS server error in which a specified plugin DLL (in registry) could not be loaded
query: any where EventID like~ (150, 770, 771)

name: SIGMA - USB Device Plugged
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects plugged/unplugged USB devices
query: any where EventID like~ (2003, 2100, 2102)

name: SIGMA - Uncommon New Firewall Rule Added In Windows Firewall Exception List
author: frack113
description: Detects when a rule has been added to the Windows Firewall exception list
query: any where (EventID like~ (2004, 2071, 2097)) and (not (Action:2 or (ApplicationPath like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*", "C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*")) or (ApplicationPath like~ ("*C:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*C:\\Temp\\*", "*C:\\Tmp\\*", "*C:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*")) or (ApplicationPath:"System" and ModifyingApplication:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\dllhost.exe") or (ModifyingApplication:"C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*" and ModifyingApplication:"*\\TiWorker.exe") or (?ApplicationPath == null))) and (not (((ModifyingApplication like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\dllhost.exe")) and ApplicationPath=="") or (((ModifyingApplication like~ ("C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\*")) and ModifyingApplication:"*\\MsMpEng.exe") or ((ApplicationPath like~ ("C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Windows Defender\\*")) and ApplicationPath:"*\\MsMpEng.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - New Firewall Rule Added In Windows Firewall Exception List For Potential Suspicious Application
author: frack113
description: Detects the addition of a new rule to the Windows Firewall exception list for an application located in a potentially suspicious location.
query: any where ((EventID like~ (2004, 2071, 2097)) and (ApplicationPath like~ ("*:\\PerfLogs\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Tmp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*"))) and (not Action:2)

name: SIGMA - New Firewall Rule Added In Windows Firewall Exception List Via WmiPrvSE.EXE
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the addition of a new "Allow" firewall rule by the WMI process (WmiPrvSE.EXE).
This can occur if an attacker leverages PowerShell cmdlets such as "New-NetFirewallRule", or directly uses WMI CIM classes such as "MSFT_NetFirewallRule".
query: any where (EventID like~ (2004, 2071, 2097)) and Action:3 and ModifyingApplication:"*:\\Windows\\System32\\wbem\\WmiPrvSE.exe"

name: SIGMA - All Rules Have Been Deleted From The Windows Firewall Configuration
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when a all the rules have been deleted from the Windows Defender Firewall configuration
query: any where (EventID like~ (2033, 2059)) and (not ModifyingApplication:"*:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe") and (not (ModifyingApplication:"*:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*" and ModifyingApplication:"*\\MsMpEng.exe*"))

name: SIGMA - A Rule Has Been Deleted From The Windows Firewall Exception List
author: frack113
description: Detects when a single rules or all of the rules have been deleted from the Windows Defender Firewall
query: any where (EventID like~ (2006, 2052)) and (not ((ModifyingApplication like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*")) or ModifyingApplication:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe" or (?ModifyingApplication == null) or ModifyingApplication=="")) and (not (ModifyingApplication:"C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*" and ModifyingApplication:"*\\MsMpEng.exe"))

name: SIGMA - The Windows Defender Firewall Service Failed To Load Group Policy
author: frack113
description: Detects activity when The Windows Defender Firewall service failed to load Group Policy
query: any where EventID:2009

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Firewall Has Been Reset To Its Default Configuration
author: frack113
description: Detects activity when Windows Defender Firewall has been reset to its default configuration
query: any where EventID like~ (2032, 2060)

name: SIGMA - Windows Firewall Settings Have Been Changed
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects activity when the settings of the Windows firewall have been changed
query: any where EventID like~ (2002, 2083, 2003, 2082, 2008)

name: SIGMA - ETW Logging/Processing Option Disabled On IIS Server
author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects changes to of the IIS server configuration in order to disable/remove the ETW logging/processing option.
query: any where (EventID:29 and Configuration:"*@logTargetW3C" and OldValue:"*ETW*") and (not NewValue:"*ETW*")

name: SIGMA - HTTP Logging Disabled On IIS Server
author: frack113
description: Detects changes to of the IIS server configuration in order to disable HTTP logging for successful requests.
query: any where EventID:29 and Configuration:"/system.webServer/httpLogging/@dontLog" and NewValue:"true"

name: SIGMA - New Module Module Added To IIS Server
author: frack113
description: Detects the addition of a new module to an IIS server.
query: any where (EventID:29 and Configuration:"*/system.webServer/modules/add*") and (not ((NewValue like~ ("AnonymousAuthenticationModule", "CustomErrorModule", "DefaultDocumentModule", "DirectoryListingModule", "FileCacheModule", "HttpCacheModule", "HttpLoggingModule", "ProtocolSupportModule", "RequestFilteringModule", "StaticCompressionModule", "StaticFileModule", "TokenCacheModule", "UriCacheModule")) or NewValue==""))

name: SIGMA - Previously Installed IIS Module Was Removed
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects the removal of a previously installed IIS module.
query: any where EventID:29 and Configuration:"*/system.webServer/modules/remove*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Active Directory Reconnaissance/Enumeration Via LDAP
author: Adeem Mawani
description: Detects potential Active Directory enumeration via LDAP
query: any where ((EventID:30 and (SearchFilter like~ ("*(groupType:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2147483648)*", "*(groupType:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2147483656)*", "*(groupType:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2147483652)*", "*(groupType:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2147483650)*", "*(sAMAccountType=805306369)*", "*(sAMAccountType=805306368)*", "*(sAMAccountType=536870913)*", "*(sAMAccountType=536870912)*", "*(sAMAccountType=268435457)*", "*(sAMAccountType=268435456)*", "*(objectCategory=groupPolicyContainer)*", "*(objectCategory=organizationalUnit)*", "*(objectCategory=nTDSDSA)*", "*(objectCategory=server)*", "*(objectCategory=domain)*", "*(objectCategory=person)*", "*(objectCategory=group)*", "*(objectCategory=user)*", "*(objectClass=trustedDomain)*", "*(objectClass=computer)*", "*(objectClass=server)*", "*(objectClass=group)*", "*(objectClass=user)*", "*(primaryGroupID=521)*", "*(primaryGroupID=516)*", "*(primaryGroupID=515)*", "*(primaryGroupID=512)*", "*Domain Admins*", "*objectGUID=\**", "*(schemaIDGUID=\*)*", "*admincount=1*"))) and (not (EventID:30 and (SearchFilter like~ ("*(domainSid=*)*", "*(objectSid=*)*"))))) or (EventID:30 and (SearchFilter like~ ("*(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=4194304)*", "*(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2097152)*", "*!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=1048574)*", "*(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=524288)*", "*(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=65536)*", "*(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=8192)*", "*(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=544)*", "*!(UserAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)*", "*msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity*", "*msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo*", "*msDS-GroupManagedServiceAccount*", "*(accountExpires=9223372036854775807)*", "*(accountExpires=0)*", "*(adminCount=1)*", "*ms-MCS-AdmPwd*"))) or (EventID:30 and SearchFilter:"(objectclass=\*)" and (DistinguishedName like~ ("*CN=Domain Admins*", "*CN=Enterprise Admins*", "*CN=Group Policy Creator Owners*")))

name: SIGMA - Standard User In High Privileged Group
author: frack113
description: Detect standard users login that are part of high privileged groups such as the Administrator group
false_positives: Standard domain users who are part of the administrator group. These users shouldn't have these right. But in the case where it's necessary. They should be filtered out using the "TargetUserName" field
query: any where (EventID:300 and TargetUserSid:"S-1-5-21-*" and (SidList like~ ("*S-1-5-32-544*", "*-500}*", "*-518}*", "*-519}*"))) and (not (TargetUserSid like~ ("*-500", "*-518", "*-519")))

name: SIGMA - ProxyLogon MSExchange OabVirtualDirectory
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects specific patterns found after a successful ProxyLogon exploitation in relation to a Commandlet invocation of Set-OabVirtualDirectory
query: any where ("OabVirtualDirectory" and " -ExternalUrl ") and ("eval(request" or "http://f/<script" or "\"unsafe\"};" or "function Page_Load()")

name: SIGMA - Certificate Request Export to Exchange Webserver
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a write of an Exchange CSR to an untypical directory or with aspx name suffix which can be used to place a webshell
query: any where ("New-ExchangeCertificate" and " -GenerateRequest" and " -BinaryEncoded" and " -RequestFile") and ("\\\\localhost\\C$" or "\\\\127.0.0.1\\C$" or "C:\\inetpub" or ".aspx")

name: SIGMA - Mailbox Export to Exchange Webserver
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Rich Warren, Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a successful export of an Exchange mailbox to untypical directory or with aspx name suffix which can be used to place a webshell or the needed role assignment for it
query: any where (("New-MailboxExportRequest" and " -Mailbox ") and ("-FilePath \"\\\\" or ".aspx")) or ("New-ManagementRoleAssignment" and " -Role \"Mailbox Import Export\"" and " -User ")

name: SIGMA - Remove Exported Mailbox from Exchange Webserver
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects removal of an exported Exchange mailbox which could be to cover tracks from ProxyShell exploit
query: any where "Remove-MailboxExportRequest" and " -Identity " and " -Confirm \"False\""

name: SIGMA - Exchange Set OabVirtualDirectory ExternalUrl Property
author: Jose Rodriguez @Cyb3rPandaH
description: Rule to detect an adversary setting OabVirtualDirectory External URL property to a script in Exchange Management log
query: any where "Set-OabVirtualDirectory" and "ExternalUrl" and "Page_Load" and "script"

name: SIGMA - MSExchange Transport Agent Installation - Builtin
author: Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the Installation of a Exchange Transport Agent
false_positives: Legitimate installations of exchange TransportAgents. AssemblyPath is a good indicator for this.
query: any where "Install-TransportAgent"

name: SIGMA - Failed MSExchange Transport Agent Installation
author: Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a failed installation of a Exchange Transport Agent
false_positives: Legitimate installations of exchange TransportAgents. AssemblyPath is a good indicator for this.
query: any where EventID:6 and Data:"*Install-TransportAgent*"

name: SIGMA - NTLM Logon
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects logons using NTLM, which could be caused by a legacy source or attackers
query: any where EventID:8002

name: SIGMA - NTLM Brute Force
author: Jerry Shockley '@jsh0x'
description: Detects common NTLM brute force device names
false_positives: Systems with names equal to the spoofed ones used by the brute force tools
query: any where EventID:8004 and (WorkstationName like~ ("Rdesktop", "Remmina", "Freerdp", "Windows7", "Windows8", "Windows2012", "Windows2016", "Windows2019"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Remote Desktop Connection to Non-Domain Host
author: James Pemberton
description: Detects logons using NTLM to hosts that are potentially not part of the domain.
false_positives: Host connections to valid domains, exclude these.
Host connections not using host FQDN.
Host connections to external legitimate domains.
query: any where EventID:8001 and TargetName:"TERMSRV*"

name: SIGMA - OpenSSH Server Listening On Socket
author: mdecrevoisier
description: Detects scenarios where an attacker enables the OpenSSH server and server starts to listening on SSH socket.
query: any where EventID:4 and process:"sshd" and payload:"Server listening on *"

name: SIGMA - Azure AD Health Monitoring Agent Registry Keys Access
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research), MSTIC
description:
This detection uses Windows security events to detect suspicious access attempts to the registry key of Azure AD Health monitoring agent.
This detection requires an access control entry (ACE) on the system access control list (SACL) of the following securable object HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Microsoft Online\Reporting\MonitoringAgent.
query: any where ((EventID like~ (4656, 4663)) and ObjectType:"Key" and ObjectName:"\\REGISTRY\\MACHINE\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Microsoft Online\\Reporting\\MonitoringAgent") and (not (ProcessName like~ ("*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Adfs.DiagnosticsAgent.exe*", "*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Adfs.InsightsService.exe*", "*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Adfs.MonitoringAgent.Startup.exe*", "*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Adfs.PshSurrogate.exe*", "*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Common.Clients.ResourceMonitor.exe*")))

name: SIGMA - Azure AD Health Service Agents Registry Keys Access
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research), MSTIC
description:
This detection uses Windows security events to detect suspicious access attempts to the registry key values and sub-keys of Azure AD Health service agents (e.g AD FS).
Information from AD Health service agents can be used to potentially abuse some of the features provided by those services in the cloud (e.g. Federation).
This detection requires an access control entry (ACE) on the system access control list (SACL) of the following securable object: HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\ADHealthAgent.
Make sure you set the SACL to propagate to its sub-keys.
query: any where ((EventID like~ (4656, 4663)) and ObjectType:"Key" and ObjectName:"\\REGISTRY\\MACHINE\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\ADHealthAgent") and (not (ProcessName like~ ("*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Adfs.DiagnosticsAgent.exe*", "*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Adfs.InsightsService.exe*", "*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Adfs.MonitoringAgent.Startup.exe*", "*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Adfs.PshSurrogate.exe*", "*Microsoft.Identity.Health.Common.Clients.ResourceMonitor.exe*")))

name: SIGMA - Powerview Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync AD Extend Right
author: Samir Bousseaden, Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g, oscd.community, Tim Shelton, Maxence Fossat
description: Backdooring domain object to grant the rights associated with DCSync to a regular user or machine account using Powerview\Add-DomainObjectAcl DCSync Extended Right cmdlet, will allow to re-obtain the pwd hashes of any user/computer
false_positives: New Domain Controller computer account, check user SIDs within the value attribute of event 5136 and verify if it's a regular user or DC computer account.
query: any where (EventID:5136 and AttributeLDAPDisplayName:"ntSecurityDescriptor" and (AttributeValue like~ ("*1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2*", "*1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2*", "*89e95b76-444d-4c62-991a-0facbeda640c*"))) and (not (ObjectClass like~ ("dnsNode", "dnsZoneScope", "dnsZone")))

name: SIGMA - AD Privileged Users or Groups Reconnaissance
author: Samir Bousseaden
description: Detect priv users or groups recon based on 4661 eventid and known privileged users or groups SIDs
false_positives: If source account name is not an admin then its super suspicious
query: any where (EventID:4661 and (ObjectType like~ ("SAM_USER", "SAM_GROUP"))) and ((ObjectName like~ ("*-512", "*-502", "*-500", "*-505", "*-519", "*-520", "*-544", "*-551", "*-555")) or ObjectName:"*admin*") and (not SubjectUserName:"*$")

name: SIGMA - ADCS Certificate Template Configuration Vulnerability
author: Orlinum , BlueDefenZer
description: Detects certificate creation with template allowing risk permission subject
false_positives: Proxy SSL certificate with subject modification
query: any where (EventID:4898 and TemplateContent:"*CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT*") or (EventID:4899 and NewTemplateContent:"*CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT*")

name: SIGMA - ADCS Certificate Template Configuration Vulnerability with Risky EKU
author: Orlinum , BlueDefenZer
description: Detects certificate creation with template allowing risk permission subject and risky EKU
false_positives: Proxy SSL certificate with subject modification
query: any where ((EventID:4898 and (TemplateContent like~ ("*1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2*", "*1.3.6.1.5.2.3.4*", "*1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.2*", "*2.5.29.37.0*"))) and TemplateContent:"*CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT*") or ((EventID:4899 and (NewTemplateContent like~ ("*1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2*", "*1.3.6.1.5.2.3.4*", "*1.3.6.1.4.1.311.20.2.2*", "*2.5.29.37.0*"))) and NewTemplateContent:"*CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT*")

name: SIGMA - Add or Remove Computer from DC
author: frack113
description: Detects the creation or removal of a computer. Can be used to detect attacks such as DCShadow via the creation of a new SPN.
query: any where EventID like~ (4741, 4743)

name: SIGMA - Access To ADMIN$ Network Share
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects access to ADMIN$ network share
query: any where (EventID:5140 and ShareName:"Admin$") and (not SubjectUserName:"*$")

name: SIGMA - AD Object WriteDAC Access
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects WRITE_DAC access to a domain object

subtechnique.name: Windows File and Directory Permissions Modification

query: any where EventID:4662 and ObjectServer:"DS" and AccessMask:"0x40000" and (ObjectType like~ ("19195a5b-6da0-11d0-afd3-00c04fd930c9", "domainDNS"))

name: SIGMA - Active Directory Replication from Non Machine Account
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects potential abuse of Active Directory Replication Service (ADRS) from a non machine account to request credentials.
query: any where (EventID:4662 and AccessMask:"0x100" and (Properties like~ ("*1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2*", "*1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2*", "*89e95b76-444d-4c62-991a-0facbeda640c*"))) and (not (SubjectUserName like~ ("*$", "MSOL_*")))

name: SIGMA - Potential AD User Enumeration From Non-Machine Account
author: Maxime Thiebaut (@0xThiebaut)
description: Detects read access to a domain user from a non-machine account
query: any where (EventID:4662 and ObjectType:"*bf967aba-0de6-11d0-a285-00aa003049e2*" and (AccessMask like~ ("*1?", "*3?", "*4?", "*7?", "*9?", "*B?", "*D?", "*F?"))) and (not (SubjectUserName like~ ("*$", "MSOL_*")))

name: SIGMA - Enabled User Right in AD to Control User Objects
author: @neu5ron
description: Detects scenario where if a user is assigned the SeEnableDelegationPrivilege right in Active Directory it would allow control of other AD user objects.
query: any where EventID:4704 and PrivilegeList:"*SeEnableDelegationPrivilege*"

name: SIGMA - Active Directory User Backdoors
author: @neu5ron
description: Detects scenarios where one can control another users or computers account without having to use their credentials.
query: any where (EventID:4738 and (not ((AllowedToDelegateTo like~ ("", "-")) or (?AllowedToDelegateTo == null)))) or ((EventID:5136 and AttributeLDAPDisplayName:"msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo") or (EventID:5136 and ObjectClass:"user" and AttributeLDAPDisplayName:"servicePrincipalName") or (EventID:5136 and AttributeLDAPDisplayName:"msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity"))

name: SIGMA - Weak Encryption Enabled and Kerberoast
author: @neu5ron
description: Detects scenario where weak encryption is enabled for a user profile which could be used for hash/password cracking.

name: SIGMA - Hacktool Ruler
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: This events that are generated when using the hacktool Ruler by Sensepost
false_positives: Go utilities that use staaldraad awesome NTLM library
query: any where (EventID:4776 and Workstation:"RULER") or ((EventID like~ (4624, 4625)) and WorkstationName:"RULER")

name: SIGMA - Remote Task Creation via ATSVC Named Pipe
author: Samir Bousseaden
description: Detects remote task creation via at.exe or API interacting with ATSVC namedpipe
query: any where EventID:5145 and ShareName:"\\\\\*\\IPC$" and RelativeTargetName:"atsvc" and AccessList:"*WriteData*"

name: SIGMA - Security Eventlog Cleared
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: One of the Windows Eventlogs has been cleared. e.g. caused by "wevtutil cl" command execution
false_positives: Rollout of log collection agents (the setup routine often includes a reset of the local Eventlog)
System provisioning (system reset before the golden image creation)
query: any where (EventID:517 and Provider_Name:"Security") or (EventID:1102 and Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-Eventlog")

name: SIGMA - Processes Accessing the Microphone and Webcam
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Potential adversaries accessing the microphone and webcam in an endpoint.
query: any where (EventID like~ (4657, 4656, 4663)) and (ObjectName like~ ("*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\CapabilityAccessManager\\ConsentStore\\microphone\\NonPackaged*", "*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\CapabilityAccessManager\\ConsentStore\\webcam\\NonPackaged*"))

name: SIGMA - CobaltStrike Service Installations - Security
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Wojciech Lesicki
description: Detects known malicious service installs that appear in cases in which a Cobalt Strike beacon elevates privileges or lateral movement
query: any where EventID:4697 and ((ServiceFileName:"*ADMIN$*" and ServiceFileName:"*.exe*") or (ServiceFileName:"*%COMSPEC%*" and ServiceFileName:"*start*" and ServiceFileName:"*powershell*") or ServiceFileName:"*powershell -nop -w hidden -encodedcommand*" or ServiceFileName like~ ("*SUVYIChOZXctT2JqZWN0IE5ldC5XZWJjbGllbnQpLkRvd25sb2FkU3RyaW5nKCdodHRwOi8vMTI3LjAuMC4xO*", "*lFWCAoTmV3LU9iamVjdCBOZXQuV2ViY2xpZW50KS5Eb3dubG9hZFN0cmluZygnaHR0cDovLzEyNy4wLjAuMT*", "*JRVggKE5ldy1PYmplY3QgTmV0LldlYmNsaWVudCkuRG93bmxvYWRTdHJpbmcoJ2h0dHA6Ly8xMjcuMC4wLjE6*"))

name: SIGMA - Failed Code Integrity Checks
author: Thomas Patzke
description: Detects code integrity failures such as missing page hashes or corrupted drivers due unauthorized modification. This could be a sign of tampered binaries.
query: any where (EventID like~ (5038, 6281)) and (not ((param1 like~ ("*\\CSFalconServiceUninstallTool_*", "*\\Program Files\\CrowdStrike\\*", "*\\System32\\drivers\\CrowdStrike\\*", "*\\Windows\\System32\\ScriptControl64_*")) or param1:"*\\Program Files\\Sophos\\*"))

name: SIGMA - DCERPC SMB Spoolss Named Pipe
author: OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Detects the use of the spoolss named pipe over SMB. This can be used to trigger the authentication via NTLM of any machine that has the spoolservice enabled.
false_positives: Domain Controllers acting as printer servers too? :)
query: any where EventID:5145 and ShareName:"\\\\\*\\IPC$" and RelativeTargetName:"spoolss"

name: SIGMA - DCOM InternetExplorer.Application Iertutil DLL Hijack - Security
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g, Open Threat Research (OTR)
description: Detects a threat actor creating a file named `iertutil.dll` in the `C:\Program Files\Internet Explorer\` directory over the network for a DCOM InternetExplorer DLL Hijack scenario.
query: any where (EventID:5145 and RelativeTargetName:"*\\Internet Explorer\\iertutil.dll") and (not SubjectUserName:"*$")

name: SIGMA - Mimikatz DC Sync
author: Benjamin Delpy, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Scott Dermott, Sorina Ionescu
description: Detects Mimikatz DC sync security events
false_positives: Valid DC Sync that is not covered by the filters; please report
Local Domain Admin account used for Azure AD Connect
query: any where (EventID:4662 and (Properties like~ ("*Replicating Directory Changes All*", "*1131f6ad-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2*", "*1131f6aa-9c07-11d1-f79f-00c04fc2dcd2*", "*9923a32a-3607-11d2-b9be-0000f87a36b2*", "*89e95b76-444d-4c62-991a-0facbeda640c*")) and AccessMask:"0x100") and (not (SubjectDomainName:"Window Manager" or (SubjectUserName like~ ("NT AUT*", "MSOL_*")) or SubjectUserName:"*$"))

name: SIGMA - Device Installation Blocked
author: frack113
description: Detects an installation of a device that is forbidden by the system policy
query: any where EventID:6423

name: SIGMA - Windows Event Auditing Disabled
author: @neu5ron, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects scenarios where system auditing (i.e.: Windows event log auditing) is disabled.
This may be used in a scenario where an entity would want to bypass local logging to evade detection when Windows event logging is enabled and reviewed.
Also, it is recommended to turn off "Local Group Policy Object Processing" via GPO, which will make sure that Active Directory GPOs take precedence over local/edited computer policies via something such as "gpedit.msc".
Please note, that disabling "Local Group Policy Object Processing" may cause an issue in scenarios of one off specific GPO modifications - however, it is recommended to perform these modifications in Active Directory anyways.

name: SIGMA - Important Windows Event Auditing Disabled
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects scenarios where system auditing for important events such as "Process Creation" or "Logon" events is disabled.

name: SIGMA - ETW Logging Disabled In .NET Processes - Registry
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Potential adversaries stopping ETW providers recording loaded .NET assemblies.
query: any where (EventID:4657 and ObjectName:"*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\.NETFramework" and ObjectValueName:"ETWEnabled" and NewValue:0) or (EventID:4657 and ObjectName:"*\\Environment*" and (ObjectValueName like~ ("COMPlus_ETWEnabled", "COMPlus_ETWFlags")) and NewValue:0)

name: SIGMA - DPAPI Domain Backup Key Extraction
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects tools extracting LSA secret DPAPI domain backup key from Domain Controllers
query: any where EventID:4662 and ObjectType:"SecretObject" and AccessMask:"0x2" and ObjectName:"*BCKUPKEY*"

name: SIGMA - DPAPI Domain Master Key Backup Attempt
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects anyone attempting a backup for the DPAPI Master Key. This events gets generated at the source and not the Domain Controller.
false_positives: If a computer is a member of a domain, DPAPI has a backup mechanism to allow unprotection of the data. Which will trigger this event.
query: any where EventID:4692

name: SIGMA - External Disk Drive Or USB Storage Device Was Recognized By The System
author: Keith Wright
description: Detects external disk drives or plugged-in USB devices.
query: any where EventID:6416 and (ClassName:"DiskDrive" or DeviceDescription:"USB Mass Storage Device")

name: SIGMA - Persistence and Execution at Scale via GPO Scheduled Task
author: Samir Bousseaden
description: Detect lateral movement using GPO scheduled task, usually used to deploy ransomware at scale
false_positives: If the source IP is not localhost then it's super suspicious, better to monitor both local and remote changes to GPO scheduled tasks.
query: any where (EventID:5136 and (AttributeLDAPDisplayName like~ ("gPCMachineExtensionNames", "gPCUserExtensionNames")) and (AttributeValue like~ ("*CAB54552-DEEA-4691-817E-ED4A4D1AFC72*", "*AADCED64-746C-4633-A97C-D61349046527*"))) or (EventID:5145 and ShareName:"*\\SYSVOL" and RelativeTargetName:"*ScheduledTasks.xml" and (AccessList like~ ("*WriteData*", "*%%4417*")))

name: SIGMA - Hidden Local User Creation
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of a local hidden user account which should not happen for event ID 4720.
query: any where (EventID:4720 and TargetUserName:"*$") and (not TargetUserName:"HomeGroupUser$")

name: SIGMA - HackTool - EDRSilencer Execution - Filter Added
author: Thodoris Polyzos (@SmoothDeploy)
description: Detects execution of EDRSilencer, a tool that abuses the Windows Filtering Platform (WFP) to block the outbound traffic of running EDR agents based on specific hardcoded filter names.
query: any where (EventID like~ (5441, 5447)) and FilterName:"*Custom Outbound Filter*"

name: SIGMA - HackTool - NoFilter Execution
author: Stamatis Chatzimangou (st0pp3r)
description: Detects execution of NoFilter, a tool for abusing the Windows Filtering Platform for privilege escalation via hardcoded policy name indicators
query: any where (EventID:5447 and FilterName:"*RonPolicy*") or (EventID:5449 and ProviderContextName:"*RonPolicy*")

name: SIGMA - HybridConnectionManager Service Installation
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Rule to detect the Hybrid Connection Manager service installation.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Hybrid Connection Manager via Azure function apps.
query: any where EventID:4697 and ServiceName:"HybridConnectionManager" and ServiceFileName:"*HybridConnectionManager*"

name: SIGMA - Impacket PsExec Execution
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects execution of Impacket's psexec.py.
query: any where EventID:5145 and ShareName:"\\\\\*\\IPC$" and (RelativeTargetName like~ ("*RemCom_stdin*", "*RemCom_stdout*", "*RemCom_stderr*"))

name: SIGMA - Possible Impacket SecretDump Remote Activity
author: Samir Bousseaden, wagga
description: Detect AD credential dumping using impacket secretdump HKTL
query: any where EventID:5145 and ShareName:"\\\\\*\\ADMIN$" and (RelativeTargetName:"*SYSTEM32\\*" and RelativeTargetName:"*.tmp*")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation CLIP+ Launcher - Security
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Clip.exe to execute PowerShell
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName:"*cmd*" and ServiceFileName:"*&&*" and ServiceFileName:"*clipboard]::*")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Obfuscated IEX Invocation - Security
author: Daniel Bohannon (@Mandiant/@FireEye), oscd.community
description: Detects all variations of obfuscated powershell IEX invocation code generated by Invoke-Obfuscation framework from the code block linked in the references
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName regex~ "\$PSHome\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$PSHome\[" or ServiceFileName regex~ "\$ShellId\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$ShellId\[" or ServiceFileName regex~ "\$env:Public\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$env:Public\[" or ServiceFileName regex~ "\$env:ComSpec\[(\s*\d{1,3}\s*,){2}" or ServiceFileName regex~ "\\*mdr\*\W\s*\)\.Name" or ServiceFileName regex~ "\$VerbosePreference\.ToString\(" or ServiceFileName regex~ "\String\]\s*\$VerbosePreference")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation STDIN+ Launcher - Security
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of stdin to execute PowerShell
query: any where (EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName:"*cmd*" and ServiceFileName:"*powershell*")) and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*${input}*", "*noexit*")) and (ServiceFileName like~ ("* /c *", "* /r *"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR+ Launcher - Security
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Environment Variables to execute PowerShell
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName:"*cmd*" and ServiceFileName:"*\"set*" and ServiceFileName:"*-f*") and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*/c*", "*/r*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation COMPRESS OBFUSCATION - Security
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via COMPRESS OBFUSCATION
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName:"*new-object*" and ServiceFileName:"*text.encoding]::ascii*" and ServiceFileName:"*readtoend*") and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*system.io.compression.deflatestream*", "*system.io.streamreader*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation RUNDLL LAUNCHER - Security
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via RUNDLL LAUNCHER
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName:"*rundll32.exe*" and ServiceFileName:"*shell32.dll*" and ServiceFileName:"*shellexec_rundll*" and ServiceFileName:"*powershell*")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Stdin - Security
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via Stdin in Scripts
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName:"*set*" and ServiceFileName:"*&&*") and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*environment*", "*invoke*", "*${input)*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Clip - Security
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Clip.exe in Scripts
query: any where EventID:4697 and ServiceFileName:"*(Clipboard|i*"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA - Security
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use MSHTA in Scripts
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName:"*mshta*" and ServiceFileName:"*vbscript:createobject*" and ServiceFileName:"*.run*" and ServiceFileName:"*window.close*")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Rundll32 - Security
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Rundll32 in Scripts
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName:"*&&*" and ServiceFileName:"*rundll32*" and ServiceFileName:"*shell32.dll*" and ServiceFileName:"*shellexec_rundll*") and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*value*", "*invoke*", "*comspec*", "*iex*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR++ LAUNCHER OBFUSCATION - Security
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via VAR++ LAUNCHER
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName:"*&&set*" and ServiceFileName:"*cmd*" and ServiceFileName:"*/c*" and ServiceFileName:"*-f*") and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*{0}*", "*{1}*", "*{2}*", "*{3}*", "*{4}*", "*{5}*"))

name: SIGMA - ISO Image Mounted
author: Syed Hasan (@syedhasan009)
description: Detects the mount of an ISO image on an endpoint
query: any where (EventID:4663 and ObjectServer:"Security" and ObjectType:"File" and ObjectName:"\\Device\\CdRom*") and (not (ObjectName like~ ("\\Device\\CdRom0\\autorun.ico", "\\Device\\CdRom0\\setup.exe", "\\Device\\CdRom0\\setup64.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Kerberoasting Activity - Initial Query
author: @kostastsale
description:
This rule will collect the data needed to start looking into possible kerberoasting activity.
Further analysis or computation within the query is needed focusing on requests from one specific host/IP towards multiple service names within a time period of 5 seconds.
You can then set a threshold for the number of requests and time between the requests to turn this into an alert.
query: any where (EventID:4769 and Status:"0x0" and TicketEncryptionType:"0x17") and (not ((ServiceName like~ ("*krbtgt", "*$")) and TargetUserName:"*$@*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential AS-REP Roasting via Kerberos TGT Requests
author: ANosir
description:
Detects suspicious Kerberos TGT requests with pre-authentication disabled (Pre-Authentication Type = 0) and Ticket Encryption Type (0x17) i.e, RC4-HMAC.
This may indicate an AS-REP Roasting attack, where attackers request AS-REP messages for accounts without pre-authentication and attempt to crack the encrypted ticket offline to recover user passwords.
false_positives: Legacy systems or applications that legitimately use RC4 encryption
query: any where EventID:4768 and TicketEncryptionType:"0x17" and ServiceName:"krbtgt" and PreAuthType:0

name: SIGMA - Potential Kerberos Coercion by Spoofing SPNs via DNS Manipulation
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects modifications to DNS records in Active Directory where the Distinguished Name (DN) contains a base64-encoded blob
matching the pattern "1UWhRCAAAAA...BAAAA". This pattern corresponds to a marshaled CREDENTIAL_TARGET_INFORMATION structure,
commonly used in Kerberos coercion attacks. Adversaries may exploit this to coerce victim systems into authenticating to
attacker-controlled hosts by spoofing SPNs via DNS. It is one of the strong indicators of a Kerberos coercion attack,.
where adversaries manipulate DNS records to spoof Service Principal Names (SPNs) and redirect authentication requests like CVE-2025-33073.
Please investigate the user account that made the changes, as it is likely a low-privileged account that has been compromised.
query: any where ((EventID like~ (5136, 5137)) and ObjectClass:"dnsNode" and (ObjectDN:"*UWhRCA*" and ObjectDN:"*BAAAA*" and ObjectDN:"*CN=MicrosoftDNS*")) or (EventID:4662 and (AdditionalInfo:"*UWhRCA*" and AdditionalInfo:"*BAAAA*" and AdditionalInfo:"*CN=MicrosoftDNS*"))

name: SIGMA - First Time Seen Remote Named Pipe
author: Samir Bousseaden
description: This detection excludes known namped pipes accessible remotely and notify on newly observed ones, may help to detect lateral movement and remote exec using named pipes
false_positives: Update the excluded named pipe to filter out any newly observed legit named pipe
query: any where (EventID:5145 and ShareName:"\\\\\*\\IPC$") and (not (RelativeTargetName like~ ("atsvc", "samr", "lsarpc", "lsass", "winreg", "netlogon", "srvsvc", "protected_storage", "wkssvc", "browser", "netdfs", "svcctl", "spoolss", "ntsvcs", "LSM_API_service", "HydraLsPipe", "TermSrv_API_service", "MsFteWds", "sql\\query", "eventlog")))

name: SIGMA - LSASS Access From Non System Account
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects potential mimikatz-like tools accessing LSASS from non system account
query: any where ((EventID like~ (4663, 4656)) and (AccessMask like~ ("0x100000", "0x1010", "0x1400", "0x1410", "0x1418", "0x1438", "0x143a", "0x1f0fff", "0x1f1fff", "0x1f2fff", "0x1f3fff", "0x40", "143a", "1f0fff", "1f1fff", "1f2fff", "1f3fff")) and ObjectType:"Process" and ObjectName:"*\\lsass.exe") and (not (SubjectUserName:"*$" or (ProcessName like~ ("*:\\Program Files\\*", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\*")) or (ProcessName:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\wbem\\WmiPrvSE.exe" and AccessMask:"0x1410"))) and (not ProcessName:"*\\SteamLibrary\\steamapps\\*")

name: SIGMA - Credential Dumping Tools Service Execution - Security
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Detects well-known credential dumping tools execution via service execution events
false_positives: Legitimate Administrator using credential dumping tool for password recovery
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*cachedump*", "*dumpsvc*", "*fgexec*", "*gsecdump*", "*mimidrv*", "*pwdump*", "*servpw*"))

name: SIGMA - WCE wceaux.dll Access
author: Thomas Patzke
description: Detects wceaux.dll access while WCE pass-the-hash remote command execution on source host
query: any where (EventID like~ (4656, 4663)) and ObjectName:"*\\wceaux.dll"

name: SIGMA - Metasploit SMB Authentication
author: Chakib Gzenayi (@Chak092), Hosni Mribah
description: Alerts on Metasploit host's authentications on the domain.
query: any where ((EventID like~ (4625, 4624)) and LogonType:3 and AuthenticationPackageName:"NTLM" and WorkstationName regex~ "^[A-Za-z0-9]{16}$") or (EventID:4776 and Workstation regex~ "^[A-Za-z0-9]{16}$")

name: SIGMA - Metasploit Or Impacket Service Installation Via SMB PsExec
author: Bartlomiej Czyz, Relativity
description: Detects usage of Metasploit SMB PsExec (exploit/windows/smb/psexec) and Impacket psexec.py by triggering on specific service installation
false_positives: Possible, different agents with a 8 character binary and a 4, 8 or 16 character service name
query: any where (EventID:4697 and ServiceFileName regex~ "^%systemroot%\\[a-zA-Z]{8}\.exe$" and ServiceName regex~ "(^[a-zA-Z]{4}$)|(^[a-zA-Z]{8}$)|(^[a-zA-Z]{16}$)" and ServiceStartType:3 and ServiceType:"0x10") and (not ServiceName:"PSEXESVC")

name: SIGMA - Meterpreter or Cobalt Strike Getsystem Service Installation - Security
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Ecco, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of getsystem Meterpreter/Cobalt Strike command by detecting a specific service installation
query: any where EventID:4697 and (((ServiceFileName:"*/c*" and ServiceFileName:"*echo*" and ServiceFileName:"*\\pipe\\*") and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*cmd*", "*%COMSPEC%*"))) or (ServiceFileName:"*rundll32*" and ServiceFileName:"*.dll,a*" and ServiceFileName:"*/p:*") or ServiceFileName:"\\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$\\*")

name: SIGMA - NetNTLM Downgrade Attack
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), wagga
description: Detects NetNTLM downgrade attack
query: any where EventID:4657 and (ObjectName:"*\\REGISTRY\\MACHINE\\SYSTEM*" and ObjectName:"*ControlSet*" and ObjectName:"*\\Control\\Lsa*") and (ObjectValueName like~ ("LmCompatibilityLevel", "NtlmMinClientSec", "RestrictSendingNTLMTraffic"))

name: SIGMA - Windows Network Access Suspicious desktop.ini Action
author: Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO)
description: Detects unusual processes accessing desktop.ini remotely over network share, which can be leveraged to alter how Explorer displays a folder's content (i.e. renaming files) without changing them on disk.
query: any where EventID:5145 and ObjectType:"File" and RelativeTargetName:"*\\desktop.ini" and (AccessList like~ ("*WriteData*", "*DELETE*", "*WriteDAC*", "*AppendData*", "*AddSubdirectory*"))

name: SIGMA - New or Renamed User Account with '$' Character
author: Ilyas Ochkov, oscd.community
description: Detects the creation of a user with the "$" character. This can be used by attackers to hide a user or trick detection systems that lack the parsing mechanisms.
query: any where ((EventID:4720 and SamAccountName:"*$*") or (EventID:4781 and NewTargetUserName:"*$*")) and (not (EventID:4720 and TargetUserName:"HomeGroupUser$"))

name: SIGMA - Denied Access To Remote Desktop
author: Pushkarev Dmitry
description:
This event is generated when an authenticated user who is not allowed to log on remotely attempts to connect to this computer through Remote Desktop.
Often, this event can be generated by attackers when searching for available windows servers in the network.
false_positives: Valid user was not added to RDP group
query: any where EventID:4825

name: SIGMA - Password Policy Enumerated
author: Zach Mathis
description: Detects when the password policy is enumerated.
query: any where EventID:4661 and AccessList:"*%%5392*" and ObjectServer:"Security Account Manager"

name: SIGMA - Windows Pcap Drivers
author: Cian Heasley
description: Detects Windows Pcap driver installation based on a list of associated .sys files.
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*pcap*", "*npcap*", "*npf*", "*nm3*", "*ndiscap*", "*nmnt*", "*windivert*", "*USBPcap*", "*pktmon*"))

name: SIGMA - Possible PetitPotam Coerce Authentication Attempt
author: Mauricio Velazco, Michael Haag
description: Detect PetitPotam coerced authentication activity.
query: any where EventID:5145 and ShareName:"\\\\*" and ShareName:"*\\IPC$" and RelativeTargetName:"lsarpc" and SubjectUserName:"ANONYMOUS LOGON"

name: SIGMA - PetitPotam Suspicious Kerberos TGT Request
author: Mauricio Velazco, Michael Haag
description:
Detect suspicious Kerberos TGT requests.
Once an attacer obtains a computer certificate by abusing Active Directory Certificate Services in combination with PetitPotam, the next step would be to leverage the certificate for malicious purposes.
One way of doing this is to request a Kerberos Ticket Granting Ticket using a tool like Rubeus.
This request will generate a 4768 event with some unusual fields depending on the environment.
This analytic will require tuning, we recommend filtering Account_Name to the Domain Controller computer accounts.
false_positives: False positives are possible if the environment is using certificates for authentication. We recommend filtering Account_Name to the Domain Controller computer accounts.
query: any where (EventID:4768 and TargetUserName:"*$" and CertThumbprint:"*") and (not (IpAddress=="::1" or CertThumbprint==""))

name: SIGMA - Possible DC Shadow Attack
author: Ilyas Ochkov, oscd.community, Chakib Gzenayi (@Chak092), Hosni Mribah
description: Detects DCShadow via create new SPN
false_positives: Valid on domain controllers; exclude known DCs
query: any where (EventID:4742 and ServicePrincipalNames:"*GC/*") or (EventID:5136 and AttributeLDAPDisplayName:"servicePrincipalName" and AttributeValue:"GC/*")

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Scripts Installed as Services - Security
author: oscd.community, Natalia Shornikova
description: Detects powershell script installed as a Service
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceFileName like~ ("*powershell*", "*pwsh*"))

name: SIGMA - Protected Storage Service Access
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects access to a protected_storage service over the network. Potential abuse of DPAPI to extract domain backup keys from Domain Controllers
query: any where EventID:5145 and ShareName:"*IPC*" and RelativeTargetName:"protected_storage"

name: SIGMA - RDP over Reverse SSH Tunnel WFP
author: Samir Bousseaden
description: Detects svchost hosting RDP termsvcs communicating with the loopback address
false_positives: Programs that connect locally to the RDP port
query: any where EventID:5156 and ((SourcePort:3389 and (DestAddress like~ ("127.*", "::1"))) or (DestPort:3389 and (SourceAddress like~ ("127.*", "::1")))) and (not (FilterOrigin:"AppContainer Loopback" or (Application like~ ("*\\thor.exe", "*\\thor64.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Register new Logon Process by Rubeus
author: Roberto Rodriguez (source), Ilyas Ochkov (rule), oscd.community
description: Detects potential use of Rubeus via registered new trusted logon process
query: any where EventID:4611 and LogonProcessName:"User32LogonProcesss"

name: SIGMA - Service Registry Key Read Access Request
author: Center for Threat Informed Defense (CTID) Summiting the Pyramid Team
description:
Detects "read access" requests on the services registry key.
Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the Registry entries used by services.
Adversaries may use flaws in the permissions for Registry keys related to services to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code when a service starts.
false_positives: Likely from legitimate applications reading their key. Requires heavy tuning
query: any where EventID:4663 and (ObjectName:"*\\SYSTEM\\*" and ObjectName:"*ControlSet\\Services\\*") and AccessList:"*%%1538*"

name: SIGMA - Remote PowerShell Sessions Network Connections (WinRM)
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects basic PowerShell Remoting (WinRM) by monitoring for network inbound connections to ports 5985 OR 5986
false_positives: Legitimate use of remote PowerShell execution
query: any where EventID:5156 and (DestPort like~ (5985, 5986)) and LayerRTID:44

name: SIGMA - Replay Attack Detected
author: frack113
description: Detects possible Kerberos Replay Attack on the domain controllers when "KRB_AP_ERR_REPEAT" Kerberos response is sent to the client
query: any where EventID:4649

name: SIGMA - SAM Registry Hive Handle Request
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects handles requested to SAM registry hive
query: any where EventID:4656 and ObjectType:"Key" and ObjectName:"*\\SAM"

name: SIGMA - SCM Database Handle Failure
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects non-system users failing to get a handle of the SCM database.
query: any where (EventID:4656 and ObjectType:"SC_MANAGER OBJECT" and ObjectName:"ServicesActive" and AccessMask:"0xf003f") and (not SubjectLogonId:"0x3e4")

name: SIGMA - SCM Database Privileged Operation
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g, Tim Shelton
description: Detects non-system users performing privileged operation os the SCM database
query: any where (EventID:4674 and ObjectType:"SC_MANAGER OBJECT" and ObjectName:"servicesactive" and PrivilegeList:"SeTakeOwnershipPrivilege") and (not (SubjectLogonId:"0x3e4" and ProcessName:"*:\\Windows\\System32\\services.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Secure Deletion with SDelete
author: Thomas Patzke
description: Detects files that have extensions commonly seen while SDelete is used to wipe files.
false_positives: Files that are interacted with that have these extensions legitimately
query: any where (EventID like~ (4656, 4663, 4658)) and (ObjectName like~ ("*.AAA", "*.ZZZ"))

name: SIGMA - Service Installed By Unusual Client - Security
author: Tim Rauch (Nextron Systems), Elastic (idea)
description: Detects a service installed by a client which has PID 0 or whose parent has PID 0
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ClientProcessId:0 or ParentProcessId:0)

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool Services Have Been Installed - Security
author: Connor Martin, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects service installation of different remote access tools software. These software are often abused by threat actors to perform
false_positives: The rule doesn't look for anything suspicious so false positives are expected. If you use one of the tools mentioned, comment it out
query: any where EventID:4697 and (ServiceName like~ ("*AmmyyAdmin*", "*AnyDesk*", "*Atera*", "*BASupportExpressSrvcUpdater*", "*BASupportExpressStandaloneService*", "*chromoting*", "*GoToAssist*", "*GoToMyPC*", "*jumpcloud*", "*LMIGuardianSvc*", "*LogMeIn*", "*monblanking*", "*Parsec*", "*RManService*", "*RPCPerformanceService*", "*RPCService*", "*SplashtopRemoteService*", "*SSUService*", "*TeamViewer*", "*TightVNC*", "*vncserver*", "*Zoho*"))

name: SIGMA - SMB Create Remote File Admin Share
author: Jose Rodriguez (@Cyb3rPandaH), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Look for non-system accounts SMB accessing a file with write (0x2) access mask via administrative share (i.e C$).
query: any where (EventID:5145 and ShareName:"*C$" and AccessMask:"0x2") and (not SubjectUserName:"*$") and (not IpAddress=="::1")

name: SIGMA - A New Trust Was Created To A Domain
author: Thomas Patzke
description: Addition of domains is seldom and should be verified for legitimacy.
query: any where EventID:4706

name: SIGMA - Addition of SID History to Active Directory Object
author: Thomas Patzke, @atc_project (improvements)
description: An attacker can use the SID history attribute to gain additional privileges.
false_positives: Migration of an account into a new domain
query: any where (EventID like~ (4765, 4766)) or (EventID:4738 and (not (SidHistory like~ ("-", "%%1793"))) and ?SidHistory != null)

name: SIGMA - Win Susp Computer Name Containing Samtheadmin
author: elhoim
description: Detects suspicious computer name samtheadmin-{1..100}$ generated by hacktool
query: any where (SamAccountName:"SAMTHEADMIN-*" and SamAccountName:"*$") or (TargetUserName:"SAMTHEADMIN-*" and TargetUserName:"*$")

name: SIGMA - Password Change on Directory Service Restore Mode (DSRM) Account
author: Thomas Patzke
description:
Detects potential attempts made to set the Directory Services Restore Mode administrator password.
The Directory Service Restore Mode (DSRM) account is a local administrator account on Domain Controllers.
Attackers may change the password in order to obtain persistence.
false_positives: Initial installation of a domain controller.
query: any where EventID:4794

name: SIGMA - Account Tampering - Suspicious Failed Logon Reasons
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: This method uses uncommon error codes on failed logons to determine suspicious activity and tampering with accounts that have been disabled or somehow restricted.

name: SIGMA - Group Policy Abuse for Privilege Addition
author: Elastic, Josh Nickels, Marius Rothenbücher
description: Detects the first occurrence of a modification to Group Policy Object Attributes to add privileges to user accounts or use them to add users as local admins.
false_positives: Users allowed to perform these modifications (user found in field SubjectUserName)
query: any where EventID:5136 and AttributeLDAPDisplayName:"gPCMachineExtensionNames" and (AttributeValue like~ ("*827D319E-6EAC-11D2-A4EA-00C04F79F83A*", "*803E14A0-B4FB-11D0-A0D0-00A0C90F574B*"))

name: SIGMA - Startup/Logon Script Added to Group Policy Object
author: Elastic, Josh Nickels, Marius Rothenbücher
description: Detects the modification of Group Policy Objects (GPO) to add a startup/logon script to users or computer objects.
query: any where (EventID like~ (5136, 5145)) and ((((AttributeLDAPDisplayName like~ ("gPCMachineExtensionNames", "gPCUserExtensionNames")) and AttributeValue:"*42B5FAAE-6536-11D2-AE5A-0000F87571E3*") and (AttributeValue like~ ("*40B6664F-4972-11D1-A7CA-0000F87571E3*", "*40B66650-4972-11D1-A7CA-0000F87571E3*"))) or (ShareName:"*\\SYSVOL" and (RelativeTargetName like~ ("*\\scripts.ini", "*\\psscripts.ini")) and AccessList:"*%%4417*"))

name: SIGMA - Kerberos Manipulation
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects failed Kerberos TGT issue operation. This can be a sign of manipulations of TGT messages by an attacker.

name: SIGMA - Suspicious LDAP-Attributes Used
author: xknow @xknow_infosec
description: Detects the usage of particular AttributeLDAPDisplayNames, which are known for data exchange via LDAP by the tool LDAPFragger and are additionally not commonly used in companies.
false_positives: Companies, who may use these default LDAP-Attributes for personal information
query: any where EventID:5136 and AttributeValue:"*" and (AttributeLDAPDisplayName like~ ("primaryInternationalISDNNumber", "otherFacsimileTelephoneNumber", "primaryTelexNumber"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Windows ANONYMOUS LOGON Local Account Created
author: James Pemberton / @4A616D6573
description: Detects the creation of suspicious accounts similar to ANONYMOUS LOGON, such as using additional spaces. Created as an covering detection for exclusion of Logon Type 3 from ANONYMOUS LOGON accounts.
query: any where EventID:4720 and (SamAccountName:"*ANONYMOUS*" and SamAccountName:"*LOGON*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Remote Logon with Explicit Credentials
author: oscd.community, Teymur Kheirkhabarov @HeirhabarovT, Zach Stanford @svch0st, Tim Shelton
description: Detects suspicious processes logging on with explicit credentials
false_positives: Administrators that use the RunAS command or scheduled tasks
query: any where (EventID:4648 and (ProcessName like~ ("*\\cmd.exe", "*\\powershell.exe", "*\\pwsh.exe", "*\\winrs.exe", "*\\wmic.exe", "*\\net.exe", "*\\net1.exe", "*\\reg.exe"))) and (not (TargetServerName:"localhost" or (SubjectUserName:"*$" and TargetUserName:"*$")))

name: SIGMA - Password Dumper Activity on LSASS
author: sigma
description: Detects process handle on LSASS process with certain access mask and object type SAM_DOMAIN
query: any where EventID:4656 and ProcessName:"*\\lsass.exe" and AccessMask:"0x705" and ObjectType:"SAM_DOMAIN"

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious AccessMask Requested From LSASS
author: Roberto Rodriguez, Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Dimitrios Slamaris, Mark Russinovich, Aleksey Potapov, oscd.community (update)
description: Detects process handle on LSASS process with certain access mask
false_positives: Legitimate software accessing LSASS process for legitimate reason; update the whitelist with it
query: any where ((EventID:4656 and ObjectName:"*\\lsass.exe" and (AccessMask like~ ("*0x40*", "*0x1400*", "*0x100000*", "*0x1410*", "*0x1010*", "*0x1438*", "*0x143a*", "*0x1418*", "*0x1f0fff*", "*0x1f1fff*", "*0x1f2fff*", "*0x1f3fff*"))) or (EventID:4663 and ObjectName:"*\\lsass.exe" and (AccessList like~ ("*4484*", "*4416*")))) and (not (((ProcessName like~ ("*\\csrss.exe", "*\\GamingServices.exe", "*\\lsm.exe", "*\\MicrosoftEdgeUpdate.exe", "*\\minionhost.exe", "*\\MRT.exe", "*\\MsMpEng.exe", "*\\perfmon.exe", "*\\procexp.exe", "*\\procexp64.exe", "*\\svchost.exe", "*\\taskmgr.exe", "*\\thor.exe", "*\\thor64.exe", "*\\vmtoolsd.exe", "*\\VsTskMgr.exe", "*\\wininit.exe", "*\\wmiprvse.exe", "*RtkAudUService64")) and (ProcessName like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\*", "*:\\Program Files\\*", "*:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Platform\\*", "*:\\Windows\\SysNative\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\*", "*:\\Windows\\SysWow64\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\asgard2-agent\\*"))) or ProcessName:"*:\\Program Files*" or (ProcessName like~ ("*:\\Windows\\System32\\taskhostw.exe", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\msiexec.exe", "*:\\Windows\\CCM\\CcmExec.exe")) or (ProcessName:"*:\\Windows\\Sysmon64.exe" and AccessList:"*%%4484*") or (ProcessName:"*:\\Windows\\Temp\\asgard2-agent-sc\\aurora\\*" and ProcessName:"*\\aurora-agent-64.exe" and AccessList:"*%%4484*") or (ProcessName:"*\\x64\\SCENARIOENGINE.EXE" and AccessList:"*%%4484*") or ((ProcessName:"*:\\Users\\*" and ProcessName:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\is-*") and ProcessName:"*\\avira_system_speedup.tmp" and AccessList:"*%%4484*") or (ProcessName:"*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*" and ProcessName:"*\\avira_speedup_setup_update.tmp" and AccessList:"*%%4484*") or (ProcessName:"*:\\Windows\\System32\\snmp.exe" and AccessList:"*%%4484*") or (ProcessName:"*:\\Windows\\SystemTemp\\*" and ProcessName:"*\\GoogleUpdate.exe" and AccessList:"*%%4484*"))) and (not ((ProcessName like~ ("*\\procmon64.exe", "*\\procmon.exe")) and AccessList:"*%%4484*"))

name: SIGMA - Reconnaissance Activity
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jack Croock (method), Jonhnathan Ribeiro (improvements), oscd.community
description: Detects activity as "net user administrator /domain" and "net group domain admins /domain"
query: any where EventID:4661 and AccessMask:"0x2d" and (ObjectType like~ ("SAM_USER", "SAM_GROUP")) and ObjectName:"S-1-5-21-*" and (ObjectName like~ ("*-500", "*-512"))

name: SIGMA - Password Protected ZIP File Opened
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the extraction of password protected ZIP archives. See the filename variable for more details on which file has been opened.
false_positives: Legitimate used of encrypted ZIP files
query: any where (EventID:5379 and TargetName:"*Microsoft_Windows_Shell_ZipFolder:filename*") and (not TargetName:"*\\Temporary Internet Files\\Content.Outlook*")

name: SIGMA - Password Protected ZIP File Opened (Suspicious Filenames)
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the extraction of password protected ZIP archives with suspicious file names. See the filename variable for more details on which file has been opened.
false_positives: Legitimate used of encrypted ZIP files
query: any where (EventID:5379 and TargetName:"*Microsoft_Windows_Shell_ZipFolder:filename*") and (TargetName like~ ("*invoice*", "*new order*", "*rechnung*", "*factura*", "*delivery*", "*purchase*", "*order*", "*payment*"))

name: SIGMA - Password Protected ZIP File Opened (Email Attachment)
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the extraction of password protected ZIP archives. See the filename variable for more details on which file has been opened.
false_positives: Legitimate used of encrypted ZIP files
query: any where EventID:5379 and (TargetName:"*Microsoft_Windows_Shell_ZipFolder:filename*" and TargetName:"*\\Temporary Internet Files\\Content.Outlook*")

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Outbound Kerberos Connection - Security
author: Ilyas Ochkov, oscd.community
description: Detects uncommon outbound network activity via Kerberos default port indicating possible lateral movement or first stage PrivEsc via delegation.
false_positives: Web Browsers and third party application might generate similar activity. An initial baseline is required.
query: any where (EventID:5156 and DestPort:88) and (not ((Application like~ ("\\device\\harddiskvolume*", "C:*")) and Application:"*\\Windows\\System32\\lsass.exe")) and (not (((Application like~ ("\\device\\harddiskvolume*", "C:*")) and (Application like~ ("*\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe", "*\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe"))) or ((Application like~ ("\\device\\harddiskvolume*", "C:*")) and (Application like~ ("*\\Program Files (x86)\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe", "*\\Program Files\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe"))) or Application:"*\\tomcat\\bin\\tomcat8.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Possible Shadow Credentials Added
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Elastic (idea)
description: Detects possible addition of shadow credentials to an active directory object.
false_positives: Modifications in the msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute can be done legitimately by the Azure AD Connect synchronization account or the ADFS service account. These accounts can be added as Exceptions. (From elastic FP section)
query: any where EventID:5136 and AttributeLDAPDisplayName:"msDS-KeyCredentialLink"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious PsExec Execution
author: Samir Bousseaden
description: detects execution of psexec or paexec with renamed service name, this rule helps to filter out the noise if psexec is used for legit purposes or if attacker uses a different psexec client other than sysinternal one
