query: any where (EventID:5145 and ShareName:"\\\\\*\\IPC$" and (RelativeTargetName like~ ("*-stdin", "*-stdout", "*-stderr"))) and (not RelativeTargetName:"PSEXESVC*")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Access to Sensitive File Extensions
author: Samir Bousseaden
description: Detects known sensitive file extensions accessed on a network share
false_positives: Help Desk operator doing backup or re-imaging end user machine or backup software
Users working with these data types or exchanging message files
query: any where EventID:5145 and (RelativeTargetName like~ ("*.bak", "*.dmp", "*.edb", "*.kirbi", "*.msg", "*.nsf", "*.nst", "*.oab", "*.ost", "*.pst", "*.rdp"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Kerberos RC4 Ticket Encryption
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects service ticket requests using RC4 encryption type
false_positives: Service accounts used on legacy systems (e.g. NetApp)
Windows Domains with DFL 2003 and legacy systems
query: any where (EventID:4769 and TicketOptions:"0x40810000" and TicketEncryptionType:"0x17") and (not ServiceName:"*$")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Scheduled Task Creation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious scheduled task creation events. Based on attributes such as paths, commands line flags, etc.
query: any where EventID:4698 and (TaskContent like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\WINDOWS\\Temp\\*", "*C:\\Temp\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet*", "*C:\\ProgramData\\*", "*C:\\Perflogs\\*")) and (TaskContent like~ ("*regsvr32*", "*rundll32*", "*cmd.exe</Command>*", "*cmd</Command>*", "*<Arguments>/c *", "*<Arguments>/k *", "*<Arguments>/r *", "*powershell*", "*pwsh*", "*mshta*", "*wscript*", "*cscript*", "*certutil*", "*bitsadmin*", "*bash.exe*", "*bash *", "*scrcons*", "*wmic *", "*wmic.exe*", "*forfiles*", "*scriptrunner*", "*hh.exe*"))

name: SIGMA - Important Scheduled Task Deleted/Disabled
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when adversaries stop services or processes by deleting or disabling their respective scheduled tasks in order to conduct data destructive activities
query: any where ((EventID like~ (4699, 4701)) and (TaskName like~ ("*\\Windows\\SystemRestore\\SR*", "*\\Windows\\Windows Defender\\*", "*\\Windows\\BitLocker*", "*\\Windows\\WindowsBackup\\*", "*\\Windows\\WindowsUpdate\\*", "*\\Windows\\UpdateOrchestrator\\Schedule*", "*\\Windows\\ExploitGuard*"))) and (not (EventID:4699 and SubjectUserName:"*$" and TaskName:"*\\Windows\\Windows Defender\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Scheduled Task Update
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects update to a scheduled task event that contain suspicious keywords.
query: any where EventID:4702 and (TaskContentNew like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\WINDOWS\\Temp\\*", "*C:\\Temp\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Temporary Internet*", "*C:\\ProgramData\\*", "*C:\\Perflogs\\*")) and (TaskContentNew like~ ("*regsvr32*", "*rundll32*", "*cmd.exe</Command>*", "*cmd</Command>*", "*<Arguments>/c *", "*<Arguments>/k *", "*<Arguments>/r *", "*powershell*", "*pwsh*", "*mshta*", "*wscript*", "*cscript*", "*certutil*", "*bitsadmin*", "*bash.exe*", "*bash *", "*scrcons*", "*wmic *", "*wmic.exe*", "*forfiles*", "*scriptrunner*", "*hh.exe*"))

name: SIGMA - Unauthorized System Time Modification
author: @neu5ron
description: Detect scenarios where a potentially unauthorized application or user is modifying the system time.
false_positives: HyperV or other virtualization technologies with binary not listed in filter portion of detection
references: Private Cuckoo Sandbox (from many years ago, no longer have hash, NDA as well)
query: any where EventID:4616 and (not ((ProcessName like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\VMware\\VMware Tools\\vmtoolsd.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\VBoxService.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\oobe\\msoobe.exe")) or (ProcessName:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe" and SubjectUserSid:"S-1-5-19")))

name: SIGMA - Remote Service Activity via SVCCTL Named Pipe
author: Samir Bousseaden
description: Detects remote service activity via remote access to the svcctl named pipe
query: any where EventID:5145 and ShareName:"\\\\\*\\IPC$" and RelativeTargetName:"svcctl" and AccessList:"*WriteData*"

name: SIGMA - SysKey Registry Keys Access
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description: Detects handle requests and access operations to specific registry keys to calculate the SysKey
query: any where (EventID like~ (4656, 4663)) and ObjectType:"key" and (ObjectName like~ ("*lsa\\JD", "*lsa\\GBG", "*lsa\\Skew1", "*lsa\\Data"))

name: SIGMA - Sysmon Channel Reference Deletion
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Potential threat actor tampering with Sysmon manifest and eventually disabling it
query: any where (EventID:4657 and (ObjectName like~ ("*WINEVT\\Publishers\\{5770385f-c22a-43e0-bf4c-06f5698ffbd9}*", "*WINEVT\\Channels\\Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational*")) and ObjectValueName:"Enabled" and NewValue:0) or (EventID:4663 and (ObjectName like~ ("*WINEVT\\Publishers\\{5770385f-c22a-43e0-bf4c-06f5698ffbd9}*", "*WINEVT\\Channels\\Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational*")) and AccessMask:"0x10000")

name: SIGMA - Tap Driver Installation - Security
author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Ian Davis, oscd.community
description: Detects the installation of a well-known TAP driver service. This could be a sign of potential preparation for data exfiltration using tunnelling techniques.
query: any where EventID:4697 and ServiceFileName:"*tap0901*"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Teams Application Related ObjectAcess Event
author: @SerkinValery
description: Detects an access to authentication tokens and accounts of Microsoft Teams desktop application.
query: any where (EventID:4663 and (ObjectName like~ ("*\\Microsoft\\Teams\\Cookies*", "*\\Microsoft\\Teams\\Local Storage\\leveldb*"))) and (not ProcessName:"*\\Microsoft\\Teams\\current\\Teams.exe*")

name: SIGMA - Transferring Files with Credential Data via Network Shares
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, oscd.community
description: Transferring files with well-known filenames (sensitive files with credential data) using network shares
false_positives: Transferring sensitive files for legitimate administration work by legitimate administrator
query: any where EventID:5145 and ((RelativeTargetName like~ ("*\\mimidrv*", "*\\lsass*", "*\\windows\\minidump\\*", "*\\hiberfil*", "*\\sqldmpr*")) or (RelativeTargetName like~ ("Windows\\NTDS\\ntds.dit", "Windows\\System32\\config\\SAM", "Windows\\System32\\config\\SECURITY", "Windows\\System32\\config\\SYSTEM")))

name: SIGMA - User Added to Local Administrator Group
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the addition of a new member to the local administrator group, which could be legitimate activity or a sign of privilege escalation activity
query: any where (EventID:4732 and (TargetUserName:"Administr*" or TargetSid:"S-1-5-32-544")) and (not SubjectUserName:"*$")

name: SIGMA - User Couldn't Call a Privileged Service 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess'
author: Roberto Rodriguez (source), Ilyas Ochkov (rule), oscd.community
description: The 'LsaRegisterLogonProcess' function verifies that the application making the function call is a logon process by checking that it has the SeTcbPrivilege privilege set. Possible Rubeus tries to get a handle to LSA.
query: any where EventID:4673 and Service:"LsaRegisterLogonProcess()" and Keywords:"0x8010000000000000"

name: SIGMA - Local User Creation
author: Patrick Bareiss
description: Detects local user creation on Windows servers, which shouldn't happen in an Active Directory environment. Apply this Sigma Use Case on your Windows server logs and not on your DC logs.
false_positives: Local accounts managed by privileged account management tools
query: any where EventID:4720

name: SIGMA - Potential Privileged System Service Operation - SeLoadDriverPrivilege
author: xknow (@xknow_infosec), xorxes (@xor_xes)
description:
Detects the usage of the 'SeLoadDriverPrivilege' privilege. This privilege is required to load or unload a device driver.
With this privilege, the user can dynamically load and unload device drivers or other code in to kernel mode.
This user right does not apply to Plug and Play device drivers.
If you exclude privileged users/admins and processes, which are allowed to do so, you are maybe left with bad programs trying to load malicious kernel drivers.
This will detect Ghost-In-The-Logs (https://github.com/bats3c/Ghost-In-The-Logs) and the usage of Sysinternals and various other tools. So you have to work with a whitelist to find the bad stuff.
false_positives: Other legimate tools loading drivers. Including but not limited to, Sysinternals, CPU-Z, AVs etc. A baseline needs to be created according to the used products and allowed tools. A good thing to do is to try and exclude users who are allowed to load drivers.
query: any where (EventID:4673 and PrivilegeList:"SeLoadDriverPrivilege" and Service:"-") and (not ((ProcessName like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\Dism.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\rundll32.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\fltMC.exe", "C:\\Windows\\HelpPane.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\mmc.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\wimserv.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\RuntimeBroker.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\SystemSettingsBroker.exe", "C:\\Windows\\explorer.exe")) or ProcessName:"C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft*")) and (not ((ProcessName like~ ("*\\procexp64.exe", "*\\procexp.exe", "*\\procmon64.exe", "*\\procmon.exe", "*\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Microsoft\\Teams\\current\\Teams.exe")) or ((ProcessName like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Dropbox\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Dropbox\\*")) and ProcessName:"*\\Dropbox.exe")))

name: SIGMA - User Logoff Event
author: frack113
description: Detects a user log-off activity. Could be used for example to correlate information during forensic investigations
query: any where EventID like~ (4634, 4647)

name: SIGMA - VSSAudit Security Event Source Registration
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g, Open Threat Research (OTR)
description: Detects the registration of the security event source VSSAudit. It would usually trigger when volume shadow copy operations happen.
false_positives: Legitimate use of VSSVC. Maybe backup operations. It would usually be done by C:\Windows\System32\VSSVC.exe.
query: any where AuditSourceName:"VSSAudit" and (EventID like~ (4904, 4905))

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Exclusion List Modified
author: @BarryShooshooga
description: Detects modifications to the Windows Defender exclusion registry key. This could indicate a potentially suspicious or even malicious activity by an attacker trying to add a new exclusion in order to bypass security.
query: any where EventID:4657 and ObjectName:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Exclusions\\*"

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Exclusion Registry Key - Write Access Requested
author: @BarryShooshooga, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects write access requests to the Windows Defender exclusions registry keys. This could be an indication of an attacker trying to request a handle or access the object to write new exclusions in order to bypass security.
query: any where (AccessList like~ ("*%%4417*", "*%%4418*")) and (EventID like~ (4656, 4663)) and ObjectName:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Exclusions\\*"

name: SIGMA - T1047 Wmiprvse Wbemcomn DLL Hijack
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g, Open Threat Research (OTR)
description: Detects a threat actor creating a file named `wbemcomn.dll` in the `C:\Windows\System32\wbem\` directory over the network for a WMI DLL Hijack scenario.
query: any where (EventID:5145 and RelativeTargetName:"*\\wbem\\wbemcomn.dll") and (not SubjectUserName:"*$")

name: SIGMA - WMI Persistence - Security
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin oscd.community
description: Detects suspicious WMI event filter and command line event consumer based on WMI and Security Logs.
false_positives: Unknown (data set is too small; further testing needed)
query: any where EventID:4662 and ObjectType:"WMI Namespace" and ObjectName:"*subscription*"

name: SIGMA - Locked Workstation
author: Alexandr Yampolskyi, SOC Prime
description: Detects locked workstation session events that occur automatically after a standard period of inactivity.
query: any where EventID:4800

name: SIGMA - Microsoft Defender Blocked from Loading Unsigned DLL
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects Code Integrity (CI) engine blocking Microsoft Defender's processes (MpCmdRun and NisSrv) from loading unsigned DLLs which may be an attempt to sideload arbitrary DLL
query: any where (EventID like~ (11, 12)) and (ProcessPath like~ ("*\\MpCmdRun.exe", "*\\NisSrv.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Unsigned Binary Loaded From Suspicious Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Code Integrity (CI) engine blocking processes from loading unsigned DLLs residing in suspicious locations
query: any where (EventID like~ (11, 12)) and (ImageName like~ ("*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\PerfLogs\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\TEMP\\*"))

name: SIGMA - HybridConnectionManager Service Running
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description: Rule to detect the Hybrid Connection Manager service running on an endpoint.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Hybrid Connection Manager via Azure function apps.
query: any where (EventID like~ (40300, 40301, 40302)) and ("HybridConnection" or "sb://" or "servicebus.windows.net" or "HybridConnectionManage")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Application Installed
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious application installed by looking at the added shortcut to the app resolver cache
false_positives: Packages or applications being legitimately used by users or administrators
query: any where (EventID:28115 and (Name like~ ("*Zenmap*", "*AnyDesk*", "*wireshark*", "*openvpn*"))) or (EventID:28115 and (AppID like~ ("*zenmap.exe*", "*prokzult ad*", "*wireshark*", "*openvpn*")))

name: SIGMA - Scheduled Task Executed From A Suspicious Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of Scheduled Tasks where the Program being run is located in a suspicious location or it's an unusale program to be run from a Scheduled Task
query: any where EventID:129 and (Path like~ ("*C:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*C:\\Temp\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Scheduled Task Executed Uncommon LOLBIN
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of Scheduled Tasks where the program being run is located in a suspicious location or where it is an unusual program to be run from a Scheduled Task
false_positives: False positives may occur with some of the selected binaries if you have tasks using them (which could be very common in your environment). Exclude all the specific trusted tasks before using this rule
query: any where EventID:129 and (Path like~ ("*\\calc.exe", "*\\cscript.exe", "*\\mshta.exe", "*\\mspaint.exe", "*\\notepad.exe", "*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\wscript.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Important Scheduled Task Deleted
author: frack113
description: Detects when adversaries try to stop system services or processes by deleting their respective scheduled tasks in order to conduct data destructive activities
query: any where (EventID:141 and (TaskName like~ ("*\\Windows\\SystemRestore\\SR*", "*\\Windows\\Windows Defender\\*", "*\\Windows\\BitLocker*", "*\\Windows\\WindowsBackup\\*", "*\\Windows\\WindowsUpdate\\*", "*\\Windows\\UpdateOrchestrator\\*", "*\\Windows\\ExploitGuard*"))) and (not (UserName like~ ("*AUTHORI*", "*AUTORI*")))

name: SIGMA - Ngrok Usage with Remote Desktop Service
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects cases in which ngrok, a reverse proxy tool, forwards events to the local RDP port, which could be a sign of malicious behaviour
query: any where EventID:21 and Address:"*16777216*"

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Grace Period Expired
author: Ján Trenčanský, frack113
description: Detects the expiration of the grace period of Windows Defender. This means protection against viruses, spyware, and other potentially unwanted software is disabled.
query: any where EventID:5101

name: SIGMA - LSASS Access Detected via Attack Surface Reduction
author: Markus Neis
description: Detects Access to LSASS Process
query: any where (EventID:1121 and Path:"*\\lsass.exe") and (not ((ProcessName:"C:\\Windows\\Temp\\asgard2-agent\\*" and (ProcessName like~ ("*\\thor64.exe", "*\\thor.exe"))) or (ProcessName like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\atiesrxx.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\CompatTelRunner.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\msiexec.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\nvwmi64.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\Taskmgr.exe", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\wbem\\WmiPrvSE.exe", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\msiexec.exe")) or (ProcessName like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\DriverStore\\*", "C:\\WINDOWS\\Installer\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - PSExec and WMI Process Creations Block
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects blocking of process creations originating from PSExec and WMI commands
query: any where EventID:1121 and (ProcessName like~ ("*\\wmiprvse.exe", "*\\psexesvc.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Exclusions Added
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the Setting of Windows Defender Exclusions
query: any where EventID:5007 and NewValue:"*\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Exclusions*"

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Exploit Guard Tamper
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when someone is adding or removing applications or folders from exploit guard "ProtectedFolders" or "AllowedApplications"
query: any where ((EventID:5007 and NewValue:"*\\Windows Defender\\Windows Defender Exploit Guard\\Controlled Folder Access\\AllowedApplications\\*") and (NewValue like~ ("*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\PerfLogs\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*"))) or (EventID:5007 and OldValue:"*\\Windows Defender\\Windows Defender Exploit Guard\\Controlled Folder Access\\ProtectedFolders\\*")

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Submit Sample Feature Disabled
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects disabling of the "Automatic Sample Submission" feature of Windows Defender.
query: any where EventID:5007 and NewValue:"*\\Real-Time Protection\\SubmitSamplesConsent = 0x0*"

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Malware Detection History Deletion
author: Cian Heasley
description: Windows Defender logs when the history of detected infections is deleted.
false_positives: Deletion of Defender malware detections history for legitimate reasons
query: any where EventID:1013

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Malware And PUA Scanning Disabled
author: Ján Trenčanský, frack113
description: Detects disabling of the Windows Defender feature of scanning for malware and other potentially unwanted software
query: any where EventID:5010

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender AMSI Trigger Detected
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects triggering of AMSI by Windows Defender.
query: any where EventID:1116 and SourceName:"AMSI"

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Real-time Protection Disabled
author: Ján Trenčanský, frack113
description: Detects disabling of Windows Defender Real-time Protection. As this event doesn't contain a lot of information on who initiated this action you might want to reduce it to a "medium" level if this occurs too many times in your environment
false_positives: Seen being triggered occasionally during Windows 8 Defender Updates
query: any where EventID:5001

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Real-Time Protection Failure/Restart
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock' (Update)
description: Detects issues with Windows Defender Real-Time Protection features
false_positives: Some crashes can occur sometimes and the event doesn't provide enough information to tune out these cases. Manual exception is required
query: any where (EventID like~ (3002, 3007)) and (not (Feature_Name:"%%886" and (Reason like~ ("%%892", "%%858"))))

name: SIGMA - Win Defender Restored Quarantine File
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the restoration of files from the defender quarantine
false_positives: Legitimate administrator activity restoring a file
query: any where EventID:1009

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Configuration Changes
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious changes to the Windows Defender configuration
query: any where EventID:5007 and (NewValue like~ ("*\\Windows Defender\\DisableAntiSpyware *", "*\\Windows Defender\\Scan\\DisableRemovableDriveScanning *", "*\\Windows Defender\\Scan\\DisableScanningMappedNetworkDrivesForFullScan *", "*\\Windows Defender\\SpyNet\\DisableBlockAtFirstSeen *", "*\\Real-Time Protection\\SpyNetReporting *"))

name: SIGMA - Microsoft Defender Tamper Protection Trigger
author: Bhabesh Raj, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects blocked attempts to change any of Defender's settings such as "Real Time Monitoring" and "Behavior Monitoring"
false_positives: Administrator might try to disable defender features during testing (must be investigated)
query: any where EventID:5013 and (Value like~ ("*\\Windows Defender\\DisableAntiSpyware", "*\\Windows Defender\\DisableAntiVirus", "*\\Windows Defender\\Scan\\DisableArchiveScanning", "*\\Windows Defender\\Scan\\DisableScanningNetworkFiles", "*\\Real-Time Protection\\DisableRealtimeMonitoring", "*\\Real-Time Protection\\DisableBehaviorMonitoring", "*\\Real-Time Protection\\DisableIOAVProtection", "*\\Real-Time Protection\\DisableScriptScanning"))

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Threat Detected
author: Ján Trenčanský
description: Detects actions taken by Windows Defender malware detection engines
query: any where EventID like~ (1006, 1015, 1116, 1117)

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Virus Scanning Feature Disabled
author: Ján Trenčanský, frack113
description: Detects disabling of the Windows Defender virus scanning feature
query: any where EventID:5012

name: SIGMA - WMI Persistence
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Gleb Sukhodolskiy, Timur Zinniatullin oscd.community
description: Detects suspicious WMI event filter and command line event consumer based on WMI and Security Logs.
false_positives: Unknown (data set is too small; further testing needed)
query: any where ((EventID:5861 and ("ActiveScriptEventConsumer" or "CommandLineEventConsumer" or "CommandLineTemplate")) or EventID:5859) and (not (Provider:"SCM Event Provider" and Query:"select * from MSFT_SCMEventLogEvent" and User:"S-1-5-32-544" and PossibleCause:"Permanent"))

name: SIGMA - Sysmon Application Crashed
author: Tim Shelton
description: Detects application popup reporting a failure of the Sysmon service
query: any where Provider_Name:"Application Popup" and EventID:26 and (Caption like~ ("sysmon64.exe - Application Error", "sysmon.exe - Application Error"))

name: SIGMA - NTLMv1 Logon Between Client and Server
author: Tim Shelton, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the reporting of NTLMv1 being used between a client and server. NTLMv1 is insecure as the underlying encryption algorithms can be brute-forced by modern hardware.
query: any where Provider_Name:"LsaSrv" and (EventID like~ (6038, 6039))

name: SIGMA - Active Directory Certificate Services Denied Certificate Enrollment Request
author: @SerkinValery
description:
Detects denied requests by Active Directory Certificate Services.
Example of these requests denial include issues with permissions on the certificate template or invalid signatures.
query: any where Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-CertificationAuthority" and EventID:53

name: SIGMA - DHCP Server Loaded the CallOut DLL
author: Dimitrios Slamaris
description: This rule detects a DHCP server in which a specified Callout DLL (in registry) was loaded
query: any where EventID:1033 and Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-DHCP-Server"

name: SIGMA - DHCP Server Error Failed Loading the CallOut DLL
author: Dimitrios Slamaris, @atc_project (fix)
description: This rule detects a DHCP server error in which a specified Callout DLL (in registry) could not be loaded
query: any where (EventID like~ (1031, 1032, 1034)) and Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-DHCP-Server"

name: SIGMA - Potential CVE-2021-42287 Exploitation Attempt
author: frack113
description:
The attacker creates a computer object using those permissions with a password known to her.
After that she clears the attribute ServicePrincipalName on the computer object.
Because she created the object (CREATOR OWNER), she gets granted additional permissions and can do many changes to the object.
query: any where Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-Directory-Services-SAM" and (EventID like~ (16990, 16991))

name: SIGMA - Local Privilege Escalation Indicator TabTip
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the invocation of TabTip via CLSID as seen when JuicyPotatoNG is used on a system in brute force mode
query: any where Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-DistributedCOM" and EventID:10001 and param1:"C:\\Program Files\\Common Files\\microsoft shared\\ink\\TabTip.exe" and param2:2147943140 and param3:"{054AAE20-4BEA-4347-8A35-64A533254A9D}"

name: SIGMA - Eventlog Cleared
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: One of the Windows Eventlogs has been cleared. e.g. caused by "wevtutil cl" command execution
false_positives: Rollout of log collection agents (the setup routine often includes a reset of the local Eventlog)
System provisioning (system reset before the golden image creation)
query: any where (EventID:104 and Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-Eventlog") and (not (Channel like~ ("Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational", "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational", "PowerShellCore/Operational", "Security", "System", "Windows PowerShell")))

name: SIGMA - Important Windows Eventlog Cleared
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tim Shelton, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the clearing of one of the Windows Core Eventlogs. e.g. caused by "wevtutil cl" command execution
false_positives: Rollout of log collection agents (the setup routine often includes a reset of the local Eventlog)
System provisioning (system reset before the golden image creation)
query: any where EventID:104 and Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-Eventlog" and (Channel like~ ("Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational", "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational", "PowerShellCore/Operational", "Security", "System", "Windows PowerShell"))

name: SIGMA - ISATAP Router Address Was Set
author: hamid
description:
Detects the configuration of a new ISATAP router on a Windows host. While ISATAP is a legitimate Microsoft technology for IPv6 transition, unexpected or unauthorized ISATAP router configurations could indicate a potential IPv6 DNS Takeover attack using tools like mitm6.
In such attacks, adversaries advertise themselves as DHCPv6 servers and set malicious ISATAP routers to intercept traffic.
This detection should be correlated with network baselines and known legitimate ISATAP deployments in your environment.
false_positives: Legitimate ISATAP router configuration in enterprise environments
IPv6 transition projects and network infrastructure changes
Automatic ISATAP configuration in some Windows deployments
query: any where (EventID:4100 and Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-Iphlpsvc") and (not (IsatapRouter like~ ("127.0.0.1", "::1"))) and ?IsatapRouter != null

name: SIGMA - Certificate Use With No Strong Mapping
author: @br4dy5
description:
Detects a user certificate that was valid but could not be mapped to a user in a strong way (such as via explicit mapping, key trust mapping, or a SID)
This could be a sign of exploitation of the elevation of privilege vulnerabilities (CVE-2022-34691, CVE-2022-26931, CVE-2022-26923) that can occur when the KDC allows certificate spoofing by not requiring a strong mapping.
Events where the AccountName and CN of the Subject do not match, or where the CN ends in a dollar sign indicating a machine, may indicate certificate spoofing.
false_positives: If prevalent in the environment, filter on events where the AccountName and CN of the Subject do not reference the same user
If prevalent in the environment, filter on CNs that end in a dollar sign indicating it is a machine name
query: any where (Provider_Name like~ ("Kerberos-Key-Distribution-Center", "Microsoft-Windows-Kerberos-Key-Distribution-Center")) and (EventID like~ (39, 41))

name: SIGMA - KDC RC4-HMAC Downgrade CVE-2022-37966
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the exploitation of a security bypass and elevation of privilege vulnerability with Authentication Negotiation by using weak RC4-HMAC negotiation
query: any where EventID:42 and (Provider_Name like~ ("Kerberos-Key-Distribution-Center", "Microsoft-Windows-Kerberos-Key-Distribution-Center")) and Level:2

name: SIGMA - No Suitable Encryption Key Found For Generating Kerberos Ticket
author: @SerkinValery
description:
Detects errors when a target server doesn't have suitable keys for generating kerberos tickets.
This issue can occur for example when a service uses a user account or a computer account that is configured for only DES encryption on a computer that is running Windows 7 which has DES encryption for Kerberos authentication disabled.
query: any where (Provider_Name like~ ("Kerberos-Key-Distribution-Center", "Microsoft-Windows-Kerberos-Key-Distribution-Center")) and (EventID like~ (16, 27))

name: SIGMA - Critical Hive In Suspicious Location Access Bits Cleared
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects events from the Kernel-General ETW indicating that the access bits of a hive with a system like hive name located in the temp directory have been reset.
This occurs when an application tries to access a hive and the hive has not be recognized since the last 7 days (by default).
Registry hive dumping utilities such as QuarksPwDump were seen emitting this behavior.
query: any where EventID:16 and Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-General" and (HiveName like~ ("*\\Temp\\SAM*", "*\\Temp\\SECURITY*"))

name: SIGMA - Volume Shadow Copy Mount
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g, Open Threat Research (OTR)
description: Detects volume shadow copy mount via Windows event log
false_positives: Legitimate use of volume shadow copy mounts (backups maybe).
query: any where Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-Ntfs" and EventID:98 and DeviceName:"*HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy*"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Usage of CVE_2021_34484 or CVE 2022_21919
author: Cybex
description: During exploitation of this vulnerability, two logs (Provider_Name:Microsoft-Windows-User Profiles Service) with EventID 1511 and 1515 (maybe lot of false positives with this event) are created. Moreover, it appears the directory \Users\TEMP is created may be created during the exploitation. Viewed on 2008 Server
query: any where EventID:1511 and Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-User Profiles Service"

name: SIGMA - Crash Dump Created By Operating System
author: Jason Mull
description: Detects "BugCheck" errors indicating the system rebooted due to a crash, capturing the bugcheck code, dump file path, and report ID.
query: any where Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-WER-SystemErrorReporting" and EventID:1001

name: SIGMA - Windows Update Error
author: frack113
description: Detects Windows update errors including installation failures and connection issues. Defenders should observe this in case critical update KBs aren't installed.
query: any where Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-WindowsUpdateClient" and (EventID like~ (16, 20, 24, 213, 217))

name: SIGMA - Zerologon Exploitation Using Well-known Tools
author: Demyan Sokolin @_drd0c, Teymur Kheirkhabarov @HeirhabarovT, oscd.community
description: This rule is designed to detect attempts to exploit Zerologon (CVE-2020-1472) vulnerability using mimikatz zerologon module or other exploits from machine with "kali" hostname.
query: any where (EventID like~ (5805, 5723)) and ("kali" or "mimikatz")

name: SIGMA - Vulnerable Netlogon Secure Channel Connection Allowed
author: NVISO
description: Detects that a vulnerable Netlogon secure channel connection was allowed, which could be an indicator of CVE-2020-1472.
query: any where Provider_Name:"NetLogon" and EventID:5829

name: SIGMA - NTFS Vulnerability Exploitation
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: This the exploitation of a NTFS vulnerability as reported without many details via Twitter
query: any where Provider_Name:"Ntfs" and EventID:55 and Origin:"File System Driver" and (Description:"*contains a corrupted file record*" and Description:"*The name of the file is \"\\\"*")

name: SIGMA - CobaltStrike Service Installations - System
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Wojciech Lesicki
description: Detects known malicious service installs that appear in cases in which a Cobalt Strike beacon elevates privileges or lateral movement
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and ((ImagePath:"*ADMIN$*" and ImagePath:"*.exe*") or (ImagePath:"*%COMSPEC%*" and ImagePath:"*start*" and ImagePath:"*powershell*") or ImagePath:"*powershell -nop -w hidden -encodedcommand*" or ImagePath like~ ("*SUVYIChOZXctT2JqZWN0IE5ldC5XZWJjbGllbnQpLkRvd25sb2FkU3RyaW5nKCdodHRwOi8vMTI3LjAuMC4xO*", "*lFWCAoTmV3LU9iamVjdCBOZXQuV2ViY2xpZW50KS5Eb3dubG9hZFN0cmluZygnaHR0cDovLzEyNy4wLjAuMT*", "*JRVggKE5ldy1PYmplY3QgTmV0LldlYmNsaWVudCkuRG93bmxvYWRTdHJpbmcoJ2h0dHA6Ly8xMjcuMC4wLjE6*"))

name: SIGMA - Windows Defender Threat Detection Service Disabled
author: Ján Trenčanský, frack113
description: Detects when the "Windows Defender Threat Protection" service is disabled.
false_positives: Auto updates of Windows Defender causes restarts
query: any where EventID:7036 and Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and (param1 like~ ("Windows Defender Antivirus Service", "Service antivirus Microsoft Defender")) and (param2 like~ ("stopped", "arrêté"))

name: SIGMA - smbexec.py Service Installation
author: Omer Faruk Celik
description: Detects the use of smbexec.py tool by detecting a specific service installation
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ServiceName:"BTOBTO" or (ImagePath like~ ("*.bat & del *", "*__output 2^>^&1 >*")))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation CLIP+ Launcher - System
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Clip.exe to execute PowerShell
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath:"*cmd*" and ImagePath:"*&&*" and ImagePath:"*clipboard]::*")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Obfuscated IEX Invocation - System
author: Daniel Bohannon (@Mandiant/@FireEye), oscd.community
description: Detects all variations of obfuscated powershell IEX invocation code generated by Invoke-Obfuscation framework from the code block linked in the references
query: any where EventID:7045 and (ImagePath regex~ "\$PSHome\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$PSHome\[" or ImagePath regex~ "\$ShellId\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$ShellId\[" or ImagePath regex~ "\$env:Public\[\s*\d{1,3}\s*\]\s*\+\s*\$env:Public\[" or ImagePath regex~ "\$env:ComSpec\[(\s*\d{1,3}\s*,){2}" or ImagePath regex~ "\\*mdr\*\W\s*\)\.Name" or ImagePath regex~ "\$VerbosePreference\.ToString\(" or ImagePath regex~ "\String\]\s*\$VerbosePreference")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation STDIN+ Launcher - System
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of stdin to execute PowerShell
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath:"*cmd*" and ImagePath:"*powershell*") and (ImagePath like~ ("*/c*", "*/r*"))) and (ImagePath:"*noexit*" or (ImagePath:"*input*" and ImagePath:"*$*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR+ Launcher - System
author: Jonathan Cheong, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated use of Environment Variables to execute PowerShell
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath:"*cmd*" and ImagePath:"*\"set*" and ImagePath:"*-f*") and (ImagePath like~ ("*/c*", "*/r*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation COMPRESS OBFUSCATION - System
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via COMPRESS OBFUSCATION
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath:"*new-object*" and ImagePath:"*text.encoding]::ascii*" and ImagePath:"*readtoend*") and (ImagePath like~ ("*:system.io.compression.deflatestream*", "*system.io.streamreader*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation RUNDLL LAUNCHER - System
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via RUNDLL LAUNCHER
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath:"*rundll32.exe*" and ImagePath:"*shell32.dll*" and ImagePath:"*shellexec_rundll*" and ImagePath:"*powershell*")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Stdin - System
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via Stdin in Scripts
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath:"*set*" and ImagePath:"*&&*") and (ImagePath like~ ("*environment*", "*invoke*", "*input*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Clip - System
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Clip.exe in Scripts
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and ImagePath:"*(Clipboard|i*"

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use MSHTA - System
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use MSHTA in Scripts
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath:"*mshta*" and ImagePath:"*vbscript:createobject*")

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation Via Use Rundll32 - System
author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via use Rundll32 in Scripts
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath:"*&&*" and ImagePath:"*rundll32*" and ImagePath:"*shell32.dll*" and ImagePath:"*shellexec_rundll*") and (ImagePath like~ ("*value*", "*invoke*", "*comspec*", "*iex*"))

name: SIGMA - Invoke-Obfuscation VAR++ LAUNCHER OBFUSCATION - System
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: Detects Obfuscated Powershell via VAR++ LAUNCHER
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath:"*&&set*" and ImagePath:"*cmd*" and ImagePath:"*/c*" and ImagePath:"*-f*") and (ImagePath like~ ("*{0}*", "*{1}*", "*{2}*", "*{3}*", "*{4}*", "*{5}*"))

name: SIGMA - KrbRelayUp Service Installation
author: Sittikorn S, Tim Shelton
description: Detects service creation from KrbRelayUp tool used for privilege escalation in Windows domain environments where LDAP signing is not enforced (the default settings)
query: any where EventID:7045 and ServiceName:"KrbSCM"

name: SIGMA - Credential Dumping Tools Service Execution - System
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Detects well-known credential dumping tools execution via service execution events
false_positives: Legitimate Administrator using credential dumping tool for password recovery
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath like~ ("*cachedump*", "*dumpsvc*", "*fgexec*", "*gsecdump*", "*mimidrv*", "*pwdump*", "*servpw*"))

name: SIGMA - Meterpreter or Cobalt Strike Getsystem Service Installation - System
author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Ecco, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the use of getsystem Meterpreter/Cobalt Strike command by detecting a specific service installation
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (((ImagePath:"*/c*" and ImagePath:"*echo*" and ImagePath:"*\\pipe\\*") and (ImagePath like~ ("*cmd*", "*%COMSPEC%*"))) or (ImagePath:"*rundll32*" and ImagePath:"*.dll,a*" and ImagePath:"*/p:*") or ImagePath:"\\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$\\*")

name: SIGMA - Moriya Rootkit - System
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects the use of Moriya rootkit as described in the securelist's Operation TunnelSnake report
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and ServiceName:"ZzNetSvc"

name: SIGMA - PowerShell Scripts Installed as Services
author: oscd.community, Natalia Shornikova
description: Detects powershell script installed as a Service
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath like~ ("*powershell*", "*pwsh*"))

name: SIGMA - Anydesk Remote Access Software Service Installation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the installation of the anydesk software service. Which could be an indication of anydesk abuse if you the software isn't already used.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the anydesk tool
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and ((ServiceName:"*AnyDesk*" and ServiceName:"*Service*") or ImagePath:"*AnyDesk*")

name: SIGMA - CSExec Service Installation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects CSExec service installation and execution events
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ServiceName:"csexecsvc" or ImagePath:"*\\csexecsvc.exe")

name: SIGMA - HackTool Service Registration or Execution
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects installation or execution of services
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and (EventID like~ (7045, 7036))) and ((ServiceName like~ ("*cachedump*", "*DumpSvc*", "*gsecdump*", "*pwdump*", "*UACBypassedService*", "*WCE SERVICE*", "*WCESERVICE*", "*winexesvc*")) or ImagePath:"*bypass*")

name: SIGMA - Mesh Agent Service Installation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a Mesh Agent service installation. Mesh Agent is used to remotely manage computers
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ImagePath:"*MeshAgent.exe*" or ServiceName:"*Mesh Agent*")

name: SIGMA - NetSupport Manager Service Install
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects NetSupport Manager service installation on the target system.
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ImagePath:"*\\NetSupport Manager\\client32.exe*" or ServiceName:"Client32")

name: SIGMA - PAExec Service Installation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects PAExec service installation
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ServiceName:"PAExec-*" or ImagePath:"C:\\WINDOWS\\PAExec-*")

name: SIGMA - New PDQDeploy Service - Server Side
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects a PDQDeploy service installation which indicates that PDQDeploy was installed on the machines.
PDQDeploy can be abused by attackers to remotely install packages or execute commands on target machines
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ImagePath:"*PDQDeployService.exe*" or (ServiceName like~ ("PDQDeploy", "PDQ Deploy")))

name: SIGMA - New PDQDeploy Service - Client Side
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects PDQDeploy service installation on the target system.
When a package is deployed via PDQDeploy it installs a remote service on the target machine with the name "PDQDeployRunner-X" where "X" is an integer starting from 1
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ImagePath:"*PDQDeployRunner-*" or ServiceName:"PDQDeployRunner-*")

name: SIGMA - ProcessHacker Privilege Elevation
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a ProcessHacker tool that elevated privileges to a very high level
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and ServiceName:"ProcessHacker*" and AccountName:"LocalSystem"

name: SIGMA - RemCom Service Installation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects RemCom service installation and execution events
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ServiceName:"RemComSvc" or ImagePath:"*\\RemComSvc.exe")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool Services Have Been Installed - System
author: Connor Martin, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects service installation of different remote access tools software. These software are often abused by threat actors to perform
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and (EventID like~ (7045, 7036)) and (ServiceName like~ ("*AmmyyAdmin*", "*Atera*", "*BASupportExpressSrvcUpdater*", "*BASupportExpressStandaloneService*", "*chromoting*", "*GoToAssist*", "*GoToMyPC*", "*jumpcloud*", "*LMIGuardianSvc*", "*LogMeIn*", "*monblanking*", "*Parsec*", "*RManService*", "*RPCPerformanceService*", "*RPCService*", "*SplashtopRemoteService*", "*SSUService*", "*TeamViewer*", "*TightVNC*", "*vncserver*", "*Zoho*"))

name: SIGMA - Remote Utilities Host Service Install
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Remote Utilities Host service installation on the target system.
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and ((ImagePath:"*\\rutserv.exe*" and ImagePath:"*-service*") or ServiceName:"Remote Utilities - Host")

name: SIGMA - Sliver C2 Default Service Installation
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects known malicious service installation that appear in cases in which a Sliver implants execute the PsExec commands
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ImagePath regex~ "^[a-zA-Z]:\\windows\\temp\\[a-zA-Z0-9]{10}\.exe" or (ServiceName like~ ("Sliver", "Sliver implant")))

name: SIGMA - Service Installed By Unusual Client - System
author: Tim Rauch (Nextron Systems), Elastic (idea)
description: Detects a service installed by a client which has PID 0 or whose parent has PID 0
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and ProcessId:0

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Service Installation
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious service installation commands
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath like~ ("* -nop *", "* -sta *", "* -w hidden *", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*.downloadfile(*", "*.downloadstring(*", "*\\ADMIN$\\*", "*\\Perflogs\\*", "*&&*"))

name: SIGMA - PsExec Service Installation
author: Thomas Patzke
description: Detects PsExec service installation and execution events
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ServiceName:"PSEXESVC" or ImagePath:"*\\PSEXESVC.exe")

name: SIGMA - TacticalRMM Service Installation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a TacticalRMM service installation. Tactical RMM is a remote monitoring & management tool.
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ImagePath:"*tacticalrmm.exe*" or ServiceName:"*TacticalRMM Agent Service*")

name: SIGMA - Tap Driver Installation
author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Ian Davis, oscd.community
description: Well-known TAP software installation. Possible preparation for data exfiltration using tunnelling techniques
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and ImagePath:"*tap0901*"

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Service Installation Image Path
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects uncommon service installation commands by looking at suspicious or uncommon image path values containing references to encoded powershell commands, temporary paths, etc.
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and ((ImagePath like~ ("*\\\\.\\pipe*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*", "*\\Windows\\Temp\\*")) or (ImagePath:"* -e*" and (ImagePath like~ ("* aQBlAHgA*", "* aWV4I*", "* IAB*", "* JAB*", "* PAA*", "* SQBFAFgA*", "* SUVYI*")))) and (not ImagePath:"C:\\ProgramData\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\Definition Updates\\*") and (not ImagePath:"C:\\WINDOWS\\TEMP\\thor10-remote\\thor64.exe*")

name: SIGMA - Windows Service Terminated With Error
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Windows services that got terminated for whatever reason
false_positives: False positives could occur since service termination could happen due to multiple reasons
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7023

name: SIGMA - Important Windows Service Terminated With Error
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects important or interesting Windows services that got terminated for whatever reason
false_positives: Rare false positives could occur since service termination could happen due to multiple reasons
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7023) and ((param1 like~ ("* Antivirus*", "* Firewall*", "*Application Guard*", "*BitLocker Drive Encryption Service*", "*Encrypting File System*", "*Microsoft Defender*", "*Threat Protection*", "*Windows Event Log*")) or (Binary like~ ("*770069006e0064006500660065006e006400*", "*4500760065006e0074004c006f006700*", "*6d0070007300730076006300*", "*530065006e0073006500*", "*450046005300*", "*420044004500530056004300*")))

name: SIGMA - Important Windows Service Terminated Unexpectedly
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects important or interesting Windows services that got terminated unexpectedly.
false_positives: Rare false positives could occur since service termination could happen due to multiple reasons
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7034) and (param1:"*Message Queuing*" or (Binary like~ ("*4d0053004d005100*", "*6d0073006d007100*")))

name: SIGMA - RTCore Suspicious Service Installation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the installation of RTCore service. Which could be an indication of Micro-Star MSI Afterburner vulnerable driver abuse
query: any where Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and ServiceName:"RTCore64"

name: SIGMA - Service Installation in Suspicious Folder
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects service installation in suspicious folder appdata
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045 and (ImagePath like~ ("*\\AppData\\*", "*\\\\127.0.0.1*", "*\\\\localhost*"))) and (not (ServiceName:"Zoom Sharing Service" and ImagePath:"*:\\Program Files\\Common Files\\Zoom\\Support\\CptService.exe*"))

name: SIGMA - Service Installation with Suspicious Folder Pattern
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects service installation with suspicious folder patterns
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ImagePath regex~ "^[Cc]:\\[Pp]rogram[Dd]ata\\.{1,9}\.exe" or ImagePath regex~ "^[Cc]:\\.{1,9}\.exe")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Service Installation Script
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious service installation scripts
query: any where (Provider_Name:"Service Control Manager" and EventID:7045) and (ImagePath like~ ("* -c *", "* /c *", "* –c *", "* —c *", "* ―c *") or ImagePath like~ ("* -r *", "* /r *", "* –r *", "* —r *", "* ―r *") or ImagePath like~ ("* -k *", "* /k *", "* –k *", "* —k *", "* ―k *")) and (ImagePath like~ ("*cscript*", "*mshta*", "*powershell*", "*pwsh*", "*regsvr32*", "*rundll32*", "*wscript*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential RDP Exploit CVE-2019-0708
author: Lionel PRAT, Christophe BROCAS, @atc_project (improvements)
description: Detect suspicious error on protocol RDP, potential CVE-2019-0708
query: any where (EventID like~ (56, 50)) and Provider_Name:"TermDD"

name: SIGMA - Unsigned or Unencrypted SMB Connection to Share Established
author: Mohamed Abdelghani
description:
Detects SMB server connections to shares without signing or encryption enabled.
This could indicate potential lateral movement activity using unsecured SMB shares.
false_positives: Connections from local or private IP addresses to SMB shares without signing or encryption enabled for older systems or misconfigured environments. Apply additional tuning as needed.
query: any where ((EventID:4000 and (ShareName like~ ("*IPC$*", "*ADMIN$*", "*C$*"))) and (SigningUsed:"false" or EncyptionUsed:"false")) and (not ((cidrMatch(ClientAddress, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(ClientAddress, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(ClientAddress, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(ClientAddress, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(ClientAddress, "fc00::/7")) or (ClientAddress like~ ("*00000000000000000000000000000001*", "*FE80000000000000*", "*FC00000000000000*", "*0200????7F*", "*0200????A9FE*"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Rejected SMB Guest Logon From IP
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), KevTheHermit, fuzzyf10w
description: Detect Attempt PrintNightmare (CVE-2021-1675) Remote code execution in Windows Spooler Service
false_positives: Account fallback reasons (after failed login with specific account)
query: any where EventID:31017 and UserName=="" and ServerName:"\\1*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Access Token Abuse
author: Michaela Adams, Zach Mathis
description: Detects potential token impersonation and theft. Example, when using "DuplicateToken(Ex)" and "ImpersonateLoggedOnUser" with the "LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS flag".
query: any where EventID:4624 and LogonType:9 and LogonProcessName:"Advapi" and AuthenticationPackageName:"Negotiate" and ImpersonationLevel:"%%1833"

name: SIGMA - Admin User Remote Logon
author: juju4
description: Detect remote login by Administrator user (depending on internal pattern).
query: any where EventID:4624 and LogonType:10 and AuthenticationPackageName:"Negotiate" and TargetUserName:"Admin*"

name: SIGMA - DiagTrackEoP Default Login Username
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the default "UserName" used by the DiagTrackEoP POC
query: any where EventID:4624 and LogonType:9 and TargetOutboundUserName:"thisisnotvaliduser"

name: SIGMA - A Member Was Added to a Security-Enabled Global Group
author: Alexandr Yampolskyi, SOC Prime
description: Detects activity when a member is added to a security-enabled global group
query: any where EventID like~ (4728, 632)

name: SIGMA - A Member Was Removed From a Security-Enabled Global Group
author: Alexandr Yampolskyi, SOC Prime
description: Detects activity when a member is removed from a security-enabled global group
query: any where EventID like~ (633, 4729)

name: SIGMA - Successful Overpass the Hash Attempt
author: Roberto Rodriguez (source), Dominik Schaudel (rule)
description: Detects successful logon with logon type 9 (NewCredentials) which matches the Overpass the Hash behavior of e.g Mimikatz's sekurlsa::pth module.
query: any where EventID:4624 and LogonType:9 and LogonProcessName:"seclogo" and AuthenticationPackageName:"Negotiate"

name: SIGMA - Pass the Hash Activity 2
author: Dave Kennedy, Jeff Warren (method) / David Vassallo (rule)
description: Detects the attack technique pass the hash which is used to move laterally inside the network
query: any where ((EventID:4624 and SubjectUserSid:"S-1-0-0" and LogonType:3 and LogonProcessName:"NtLmSsp" and KeyLength:0) or (EventID:4624 and LogonType:9 and LogonProcessName:"seclogo")) and (not TargetUserName:"ANONYMOUS LOGON")

name: SIGMA - Scanner PoC for CVE-2019-0708 RDP RCE Vuln
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Adam Bradbury (idea)
description: Detects the use of a scanner by zerosum0x0 that discovers targets vulnerable to  CVE-2019-0708 RDP RCE aka BlueKeep
query: any where EventID:4625 and TargetUserName:"AAAAAAA"

name: SIGMA - RDP Login from Localhost
author: Thomas Patzke
description: RDP login with localhost source address may be a tunnelled login
query: any where EventID:4624 and LogonType:10 and (IpAddress like~ ("::1", "127.0.0.1"))

name: SIGMA - A Security-Enabled Global Group Was Deleted
author: Alexandr Yampolskyi, SOC Prime
description: Detects activity when a security-enabled global group is deleted
query: any where EventID like~ (4730, 634)

name: SIGMA - External Remote RDP Logon from Public IP
author: Micah Babinski (@micahbabinski), Zach Mathis (@yamatosecurity)
description: Detects successful logon from public IP address via RDP. This can indicate a publicly-exposed RDP port.
false_positives: Legitimate or intentional inbound connections from public IP addresses on the RDP port.
query: any where (EventID:4624 and LogonType:10) and (not ((cidrMatch(IpAddress, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "fc00::/7") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "fe80::/10")) or IpAddress:"-"))

name: SIGMA - External Remote SMB Logon from Public IP
author: Micah Babinski (@micahbabinski), Zach Mathis (@yamatosecurity)
description: Detects successful logon from public IP address via SMB. This can indicate a publicly-exposed SMB port.
false_positives: Legitimate or intentional inbound connections from public IP addresses on the SMB port.
query: any where (EventID:4624 and LogonType:3) and (not ((cidrMatch(IpAddress, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "fc00::/7") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "fe80::/10")) or IpAddress:"-"))

name: SIGMA - Failed Logon From Public IP
author: NVISO
description: Detects a failed logon attempt from a public IP. A login from a public IP can indicate a misconfigured firewall or network boundary.
false_positives: Legitimate logon attempts over the internet
query: any where EventID:4625 and (not (IpAddress:"*-*" or (cidrMatch(IpAddress, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "fc00::/7") or cidrMatch(IpAddress, "fe80::/10"))))

name: SIGMA - Outgoing Logon with New Credentials
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects logon events that specify new credentials
query: any where EventID:4624 and LogonType:9

name: SIGMA - Potential Privilege Escalation via Local Kerberos Relay over LDAP
author: Elastic, @SBousseaden
description:
Detects a suspicious local successful logon event where the Logon Package is Kerberos, the remote address is set to localhost, and the target user SID is the built-in local Administrator account.
This may indicate an attempt to leverage a Kerberos relay attack variant that can be used to elevate privilege locally from a domain joined limited user to local System privileges.
query: any where (EventID:4624 and LogonType:3 and AuthenticationPackageName:"Kerberos" and IpAddress=="127.0.0.1" and TargetUserSid:"S-1-5-21-*" and TargetUserSid:"*-500") and (not IpPort:"0")

name: SIGMA - RottenPotato Like Attack Pattern
author: @SBousseaden, Florian Roth
description: Detects logon events that have characteristics of events generated during an attack with RottenPotato and the like
query: any where EventID:4624 and LogonType:3 and TargetUserName:"ANONYMOUS LOGON" and WorkstationName:"-" and (IpAddress like~ ("127.0.0.1", "::1"))

name: SIGMA - Successful Account Login Via WMI
author: Thomas Patzke
description: Detects successful logon attempts performed with WMI
query: any where EventID:4624 and ProcessName:"*\\WmiPrvSE.exe"

name: SIGMA - Windows Filtering Platform Blocked Connection From EDR Agent Binary
author: @gott_cyber
description:
Detects a Windows Filtering Platform (WFP) blocked connection event involving common Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) agents.
Adversaries may use WFP filters to prevent Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) agents from reporting security events.
query: any where EventID:5157 and (Application like~ ("*\\AmSvc.exe", "*\\cb.exe", "*\\CETASvc.exe", "*\\CNTAoSMgr.exe", "*\\CrAmTray.exe", "*\\CrsSvc.exe", "*\\CSFalconContainer.exe", "*\\CSFalconService.exe", "*\\CybereasonAV.exe", "*\\CylanceSvc.exe", "*\\cyserver.exe", "*\\CyveraService.exe", "*\\CyvrFsFlt.exe", "*\\EIConnector.exe", "*\\elastic-agent.exe", "*\\elastic-endpoint.exe", "*\\EndpointBasecamp.exe", "*\\ExecutionPreventionSvc.exe", "*\\filebeat.exe", "*\\fortiedr.exe", "*\\hmpalert.exe", "*\\hurukai.exe", "*\\LogProcessorService.exe", "*\\mcsagent.exe", "*\\mcsclient.exe", "*\\MsMpEng.exe", "*\\MsSense.exe", "*\\Ntrtscan.exe", "*\\PccNTMon.exe", "*\\QualysAgent.exe", "*\\RepMgr.exe", "*\\RepUtils.exe", "*\\RepUx.exe", "*\\RepWAV.exe", "*\\RepWSC.exe", "*\\sedservice.exe", "*\\SenseCncProxy.exe", "*\\SenseIR.exe", "*\\SenseNdr.exe", "*\\SenseSampleUploader.exe", "*\\SentinelAgent.exe", "*\\SentinelAgentWorker.exe", "*\\SentinelBrowserNativeHost.exe", "*\\SentinelHelperService.exe", "*\\SentinelServiceHost.exe", "*\\SentinelStaticEngine.exe", "*\\SentinelStaticEngineScanner.exe", "*\\sfc.exe", "*\\sophos ui.exe", "*\\sophosfilescanner.exe", "*\\sophosfs.exe", "*\\sophoshealth.exe", "*\\sophosips.exe", "*\\sophosLivequeryservice.exe", "*\\sophosnetfilter.exe", "*\\sophosntpservice.exe", "*\\sophososquery.exe", "*\\sspservice.exe", "*\\TaniumClient.exe", "*\\TaniumCX.exe", "*\\TaniumDetectEngine.exe", "*\\TMBMSRV.exe", "*\\TmCCSF.exe", "*\\TmListen.exe", "*\\TmWSCSvc.exe", "*\\Traps.exe", "*\\winlogbeat.exe", "*\\WSCommunicator.exe", "*\\xagt.exe"))

name: SIGMA - Loading Diagcab Package From Remote Path
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects loading of diagcab packages from a remote path, as seen in DogWalk vulnerability
false_positives: Legitimate package hosted on a known and authorized remote location
query: any where EventID:101 and PackagePath:"*\\\\*"

name: SIGMA - Microsoft Malware Protection Engine Crash
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: This rule detects a suspicious crash of the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine
false_positives: MsMpEng might crash if the "C:\" partition is full
query: any where Provider_Name:"Application Error" and EventID:1000 and (Data:"*MsMpEng.exe*" and Data:"*mpengine.dll*")

name: SIGMA - Potential Credential Dumping Via WER - Application
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects Windows error reporting event where the process that crashed is lsass. This could be the cause of an intentional crash by techniques such as Lsass-Shtinkering to dump credential
false_positives: Rare legitimate crashing of the lsass process
query: any where Provider_Name:"Application Error" and EventID:1000 and AppName:"lsass.exe" and ExceptionCode:"c0000001"

name: SIGMA - Ntdsutil Abuse
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential abuse of ntdsutil to dump ntds.dit database
query: any where Provider_Name:"ESENT" and (EventID like~ (216, 325, 326, 327)) and Data:"*ntds.dit*"

name: SIGMA - Dump Ntds.dit To Suspicious Location
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects potential abuse of ntdsutil to dump ntds.dit database to a suspicious location
query: any where (Provider_Name:"ESENT" and EventID:325 and Data:"*ntds.dit*") and (Data like~ ("*:\\ntds.dit*", "*\\Appdata\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\Downloads\\*", "*\\Perflogs\\*", "*\\Temp\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*"))

name: SIGMA - Audit CVE Event
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Zach Mathis
description:
Detects events generated by user-mode applications when they call the CveEventWrite API when a known vulnerability is trying to be exploited.
MS started using this log in Jan. 2020 with CVE-2020-0601 (a Windows CryptoAPI vulnerability.
Unfortunately, that is about the only instance of CVEs being written to this log.
query: any where (Provider_Name like~ ("Microsoft-Windows-Audit-CVE", "Audit-CVE")) and EventID:1

name: SIGMA - Backup Catalog Deleted
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Tom U. @c_APT_ure (collection)
description: Detects backup catalog deletions
query: any where EventID:524 and Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-Backup"

name: SIGMA - Restricted Software Access By SRP
author: frack113
description: Detects restricted access to applications by the Software Restriction Policies (SRP) policy
query: any where Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-SoftwareRestrictionPolicies" and (EventID like~ (865, 866, 867, 868, 882))

name: SIGMA - Application Uninstalled
author: frack113
description: An application has been removed. Check if it is critical.
query: any where Provider_Name:"MsiInstaller" and (EventID like~ (1034, 11724))

name: SIGMA - MSI Installation From Suspicious Locations
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects MSI package installation from suspicious locations
false_positives: False positives may occur if you allow installation from folders such as the desktop, the public folder or remote shares. A baseline is required before production use.
query: any where (Provider_Name:"MsiInstaller" and (EventID like~ (1040, 1042)) and (Data like~ ("*:\\Windows\\TEMP\\*", "*\\\\*", "*\\Desktop\\*", "*\\PerfLogs\\*", "*\\Users\\Public\\*"))) and (not (Data like~ ("*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\WinGet\\*", "*C:\\Windows\\TEMP\\UpdHealthTools.msi*")))

name: SIGMA - MSI Installation From Web
author: Stamatis Chatzimangou
description: Detects installation of a remote msi file from web.
query: any where Provider_Name:"MsiInstaller" and (EventID like~ (1040, 1042)) and Data:"*://*"

name: SIGMA - Atera Agent Installation
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects successful installation of Atera Remote Monitoring & Management (RMM) agent as recently found to be used by Conti operators
query: any where EventID:1033 and Provider_Name:"MsiInstaller" and Message:"*AteraAgent*"

name: SIGMA - MSSQL Add Account To Sysadmin Role
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when an attacker tries to backdoor the MSSQL server by adding a backdoor account to the sysadmin fixed server role
query: any where Provider_Name:"*MSSQL*" and EventID:33205 and (Data:"*object_name:sysadmin*" and Data:"*statement:alter server role [sysadmin] add member *")

name: SIGMA - MSSQL Destructive Query
author: Daniel Degasperi '@d4ns4n_'
description: Detects the invocation of MS SQL transactions that are destructive towards table or database data, such as "DROP TABLE" or "DROP DATABASE".
query: any where Provider_Name:"MSSQLSERVER$AUDIT" and EventID:33205 and (Data like~ ("*statement:TRUNCATE TABLE*", "*statement:DROP TABLE*", "*statement:DROP DATABASE*"))

name: SIGMA - MSSQL Disable Audit Settings
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when an attacker calls the "ALTER SERVER AUDIT" or "DROP SERVER AUDIT" transaction in order to delete or disable audit logs on the server
false_positives: This event should only fire when an administrator is modifying the audit policy. Which should be a rare occurrence once it's set up
query: any where Provider_Name:"*MSSQL*" and EventID:33205 and (Data like~ ("*statement:ALTER SERVER AUDIT*", "*statement:DROP SERVER AUDIT*"))

name: SIGMA - MSSQL Server Failed Logon
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), j4son
description: Detects failed logon attempts from clients to MSSQL server.
false_positives: This event could stem from users changing an account's password that's used to authenticate via a job or an automated process. Investigate the source of such events and mitigate them
query: any where Provider_Name:"*MSSQL*" and EventID:18456

name: SIGMA - MSSQL Server Failed Logon From External Network
author: j4son
description: Detects failed logon attempts from clients with external network IP to an MSSQL server. This can be a sign of a bruteforce attack.

name: SIGMA - MSSQL SPProcoption Set
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when the a stored procedure is set or cleared for automatic execution in MSSQL. A stored procedure that is set to automatic execution runs every time an instance of SQL Server is started
false_positives: Legitimate use of the feature by administrators (rare)
query: any where Provider_Name:"*MSSQL*" and EventID:33205 and (Data:"*object_name:sp_procoption*" and Data:"*statement:EXEC*")

name: SIGMA - MSSQL XPCmdshell Suspicious Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when the MSSQL "xp_cmdshell" stored procedure is used to execute commands
query: any where Provider_Name:"*MSSQL*" and EventID:33205 and (Data:"*object_name:xp_cmdshell*" and Data:"*statement:EXEC*")

name: SIGMA - MSSQL XPCmdshell Option Change
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects when the MSSQL "xp_cmdshell" stored procedure setting is changed.
false_positives: Note that since the event contain the change for both values. This means that this will trigger on both enable and disable
query: any where Provider_Name:"*MSSQL*" and EventID:15457 and Data:"*xp_cmdshell*"

name: SIGMA - Relevant Anti-Virus Signature Keywords In Application Log
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Arnim Rupp
description: Detects potentially highly relevant antivirus events in the application log based on known virus signature names and malware keywords.
false_positives: Some software piracy tools (key generators, cracks) are classified as hack tools
query: any where ("Adfind" or "ASP/BackDoor " or "ATK/" or "Backdoor.ASP" or "Backdoor.Cobalt" or "Backdoor.JSP" or "Backdoor.PHP" or "Blackworm" or "Brutel" or "BruteR" or "Chopper" or "Cobalt" or "COBEACON" or "Cometer" or "CRYPTES" or "Cryptor" or "Destructor" or "DumpCreds" or "Exploit.Script.CVE" or "FastReverseProxy" or "Filecoder" or "GrandCrab " or "HackTool" or "HKTL" or "HTool-" or "/HTool" or ".HTool" or "IISExchgSpawnCMD" or "Impacket" or "JSP/BackDoor " or "Keylogger" or "Koadic" or "Krypt" or "Lazagne" or "Metasploit" or "Meterpreter" or "MeteTool" or "mikatz" or "Mimikatz" or "Mpreter" or "MsfShell" or "Nighthawk" or "Packed.Generic.347" or "PentestPowerShell" or "Phobos" or "PHP/BackDoor " or "Potato" or "PowerSploit" or "PowerSSH" or "PshlSpy" or "PSWTool" or "PWCrack" or "PWDump" or "Ransom" or "Rozena" or "Ryzerlo" or "Sbelt" or "Seatbelt" or "SecurityTool " or "SharpDump" or "Shellcode" or "Sliver" or "Splinter" or "Swrort" or "Tescrypt" or "TeslaCrypt" or "TurtleLoader" or "Valyria" or "Webshell") and (not (("anti_ransomware_service.exe" or "Anti-Ransomware" or "Crack" or "cyber-protect-service.exe" or "encryptor" or "Keygen") or Level:4 or Provider_Name:"Microsoft-Windows-RestartManager"))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect Command Execution
author: Ali Alwashali
description: Detects command execution via ScreenConnect RMM
query: any where Provider_Name:"ScreenConnect" and EventID:200 and Data:"*Executed command of length*"

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - ScreenConnect File Transfer
author: Ali Alwashali
description: Detects file being transferred via ScreenConnect RMM
query: any where Provider_Name:"ScreenConnect" and EventID:201 and Data:"*Transferred files with action*"

name: SIGMA - Microsoft Malware Protection Engine Crash - WER
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: This rule detects a suspicious crash of the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine
false_positives: MsMpEng might crash if the "C:\" partition is full
query: any where Provider_Name:"Windows Error Reporting" and EventID:1001 and (Data:"*MsMpEng.exe*" and Data:"*mpengine.dll*")
